channel.title: Coach Bennett's Podcast
channel.itunes:author: Coach Bennett
channel.description: Coach Bennett brings the motivation and inspiration and a seemingly endless supply of useful and useless knowledge. This podcast is about running... and this podcast is not about running.

item.itunes:title: 5 Ways To Make Your Easy Runs Easier
item.title: 5 Ways To Make Your Easy Runs Easier
item.itunes:summary: Episode 140. 5 Ways To Make Your Easy Runs Easier Your easy runs are your most important runs. Why? Well, because you're going to run more easy run minutes, meters and miles than any other minutes, meters or miles. item.description:
Episode 140. 5 Ways To Make Your Easy Runs Easier

Your easy runs are your most important runs. 

Why? 

Well, because you're going to run more easy run minutes, meters and miles than any other minutes, meters or miles. And if you don't run your easy runs or don't run your easy runs the right way you'll never be able to run those speed runs and long runs as great as you could or should. 

That's why. 

And that's why I made this episode. Because if you can make your easy runs easier to run there is a better chance of you running them the right way and that means there is a better chance of you being able to run better speed runs and longs runs and if you run better easy runs and speed runs and long runs then you're going to be unstoppable. 

So, yeah, this is a great episode. Hope you love it. 

Cheers and thank you for listening. 

Coach Bennett

item.title: Stress Less
item.itunes:summary: Episode 139. Stress Less If you really want to perform your best then you're going to need to learn how to deal with, handle, mitigate, remove, lessen the negative stressors in your life. There are positive and negative stressors in your life. There always will be. (Hopefully, running is an example of a positive stressor.) And if you want to run your best, perform at your best, be your best then you need recover from those positive stressors in your life and remove or deal with the negative s...
item.description:
Episode 139. Stress Less

If you really want to perform your best then you're going to need to learn how to deal with, handle, mitigate, remove, lessen the negative stressors in your life. There are positive and negative stressors in your life. There always will be. (Hopefully, running is an example of a positive stressor.) And if you want to run your best, perform at your best, be your best then you need recover from those positive stressors in your life and remove or deal with the negative stressors in your life in a better and healthier way. 

Hoping this episode helps anyone that is dealing with stress in their lives. And everyone is dealing with stress in their lives. 

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 138. Longer Runs

Let's talk about those longer runs you do. You know what runs I'm talking about. Sometimes they are known as our long runs and sometimes they're just our longer runs. No matter what we call them or how we describe them we can all do a better job on these longer runs. So, on this episode of Coach Bennett's Podcast we'll talk about how we can set ourselves up for success and make decisions and adjustments that will help and not hinder our efforts on our longer runs. 

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

item.content:encoded:
Episode 138. Longer Runs

Let's talk about those longer runs you do. You know what runs I'm talking about. Sometimes they are known as our long runs and sometimes they're just our longer runs. No matter what we call them or how we describe them we can all do a better job on these longer runs. So, on this episode of Coach Bennett's Podcast we'll talk about how we can set ourselves up for success and make decisions and adjustments that will help and not hinder our efforts on our longer runs. 

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 137. Smart Stuff People Say About Running

In a world that seems to be giving more and more screen time to really dumb opinions and asinine commentary about running (and just about everything else for that matter) I thought it might be a great idea to celebrate some smart stuff runners say. You can look at this episode as the companion show to Episode 136 which was entitled Dumb Stuff Runners Say. As much fun as the Dumb Stuff episode was to make I got to say that this was was even more fun to make! Hope you have as much fun listening to this episode as I did rambling and ranting through it. 

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

item.title: Dumb Stuff People Say About Running
item.itunes:summary: Episode 136. Dumb Stuff People Say About Running I hear all sorts of stuff. Some of it is inspiring. Some of it is motivational. Some of it is super smart. Some of it challenges my preconceived notions. Some of it is really... really... really dumb. Well, I figured that I could save you the time figuring out that some advice is asinine and sub-moronic by calling out that stupendously stupid advice on one of my podcast episodes. This means you'll have more time in your day to do cool things an...
item.description:
Episode 136. Dumb Stuff People Say About Running

I hear all sorts of stuff. Some of it is inspiring. Some of it is motivational. Some of it is super smart. Some of it challenges my preconceived notions. Some of it is really... really... really dumb. Well, I figured that I could save you the time figuring out that some advice is asinine and sub-moronic by calling out that stupendously stupid advice on one of my podcast episodes. This means you'll have more time in your day to do cool things and learn about new subjects and expand your horizons. I'll just keep documenting the idiocy and dropping a podcast now and again telling you all about my latest collection of truly stupid pieces of advice and uneducated opinions I've come across. 

You're welcome. 

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 135. Easy Wins

We're talking about easy wins so you can end a losing streak and start a winning one. That's why we're going to talk about how we can set ourselves up for success. And that's why we're talking about why we should acknowledge and celebrate those easy wins. And that's why we're going to talk about a few different easy wins that I believe will lead to more easy wins and more confidence and more starting lines being crossed. By the way, listening to this episode of Coach Bennett's Podcast is an easy win. So, thank you for listening... you winner. 

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

item.itunes:title: Do The Right Thing
item.title: Do The Right Thing
item.itunes:summary: Episode 134. Do The Right Thing It shouldn't be surprising that doing the right thing consistently leads to good things happening consistently or at the very least you can be proud of what YOU did consistently. And this is true between the start and finish lines and beyond the start and finish lines. This episode is about the foundational pillars of what exactly "do the right thing" means for a runner or coach and how it can shape the running journey they are on. It's never not the right time...
item.description:
Episode 134. Do The Right Thing

It shouldn't be surprising that doing the right thing consistently leads to good things happening consistently or at the very least you can be proud of what YOU did consistently. And this is true between the start and finish lines and beyond the start and finish lines. This episode is about the foundational pillars of what exactly "do the right thing" means for a runner or coach and how it can shape the running journey they are on. It's never not the right time for this conversation but sometimes it's more of the right time for this conversation. This is one of those times. 

Thank you for listening to and supporting the podcast.

Coach Bennett

item.itunes:title: Right Here. Right Now.
item.title: Right Here. Right Now.
item.itunes:summary: Episode 133. Right Here. Right Now. You want to know where and when you can work on getting better? Right here and right now. That's the answer. And that's the only answer. You can't work on getting better yesterday. That starting line has left the station and it ain't coming back. And you sure can't work on getting better tomorrow. You'll be waiting forever for that starting line to show up. It's here and now that we get the work done. And it's the best time to do that work because it's the ...
item.description:
Episode 133. Right Here. Right Now.

You want to know where and when you can work on getting better? Right here and right now. That's the answer. And that's the only answer. You can't work on getting better yesterday. That starting line has left the station and it ain't coming back. And you sure can't work on getting better tomorrow. You'll be waiting forever for that starting line to show up. It's here and now that we get the work done. And it's the best time to do that work because it's the only time we get to work with. So we need to make the best of it. That's what this episode is all about. And we'll even break down what it really means to be optimistic, realistic and pessimistic with a little help from our always reliable assistant coach - the dictionary. Enjoy the episode. 

Cheers and thank you for listening to the show!

Coach Bennett

Send us a message

item.itunes:title: How To Start or Keep On Running (Using U2 Songs as Inspiration)
item.title: How To Start or Keep On Running (Using U2 Songs as Inspiration)
item.itunes:summary: Episode 132. How To Start or Keep On Running (Using U2 Songs as Inspiration) You ever wonder how you or someone you care about could best start, start over, start running again or just keep on running? And did you ever wonder about those things but also wonder if there is a way to solely use song titles from the epic and legendary Irish band U2 to serve as chapter titles for that noble and righteous quest? You too??? Get it? I'll wait. Well, the wait is over! Because that's ...
item.description:
Episode 132. How To Start or Keep On Running (Using U2 Songs as Inspiration) 

You ever wonder how you or someone you care about could best start, start over, start running again or just keep on running? And did you ever wonder about those things but also wonder if there is a way to solely use song titles from the epic and legendary Irish band U2 to serve as chapter titles for that noble and righteous quest? You too??? Get it? 

I'll wait. 

Well, the wait is over! Because that's what this episode of Coach Bennett's Podcast is all about. And there's a bonus! You get a mixtape playlist of all the U2 songs I mention in this special episode. That's right. item.title: Why I Didn't Break 4
item.itunes:summary: Episode 131. Why I Didn't Break 4 I had a few big goals when I was running and racing competitively. One of those goals was breaking 4 minutes in the mile. Well, as you can probably surmise from the title of this episode... I didn't. But my failure to break 4 has led to this soon to be very successful episode of Coach Bennett's Podcast. That is assuming all of you listen to and share the episode with everyone you know. Anyway, in the episode I break down a whole bunch of reasons why I believe...
item.description:
Episode 131. Why I Didn't Break 4

I had a few big goals when I was running and racing competitively. One of those goals was breaking 4 minutes in the mile. Well, as you can probably surmise from the title of this episode... I didn't. But my failure to break 4 has led to this soon to be very successful episode of Coach Bennett's Podcast. That is assuming all of you listen to and share the episode with everyone you know. Anyway, in the episode I break down a whole bunch of reasons why I believe I came up short. I go over training mistakes, mindset issues, poor racing strategy and so much more. There were a lot of reasons why I didn't break 4. My hope is that this episode will help you have a better chance at achieving and accomplishing your running goals. So, sit back and enjoy the episode. 

Cheers and thank you for listening. 

Coach Bennett

item.title: How To End The Year Running
item.itunes:summary: Episode 130. How To End The Year Running The end of the year is not the time that people start running. In fact, the end of the year os when most people stop running. Poor weather, less hours of sunlight, holiday obligations and travel, work and life stressors - it all adds up to less if any running getting done at all. That's why we have Episode #130! Because we're going to flip the script. We're not going to wait until the New Year to start running or start running again. This good ol...
item.description:
Episode 130. How To End The Year Running

The end of the year is not the time that people start running. In fact, the end of the year os when most people stop running. Poor weather, less hours of sunlight, holiday obligations and travel, work and life stressors - it all adds up to less if any  running getting done at all. That's why we have Episode #130! Because we're going to flip the script. We're not going to wait until the New Year to start running or start running again. This good old year we're in is good enough and so are we! So, listen up because this epsiode of Coach Bennett's Podcast is full of great running advice and tips you can use to make sure you end this year running.

Cheers and thank you for listening. 

Coach Bennett

item.title: Maybe Is A Power
item.itunes:summary: Episode 129. Maybe Is A Power I headed out on my run today and it was drizzling. I though to myself that maybe I'll run 15 minutes... but maybe I'll run 20 minutes... and maybe I'll run 25 minutes. The power here resided in the word maybe. The ownership and the excitement and the honesty and the possibility was all birthed and made possible by that word maybe. This episode is about how you can make your running better by incorporating that word... maybe.. into your training and your running a...
item.description:
Episode 129. Maybe Is A Power

I headed out on my run today and it was drizzling. I though to myself that maybe I'll run 15 minutes... but maybe I'll run 20 minutes... and maybe I'll run 25 minutes. The power here resided in the word maybe. The ownership and the excitement and the honesty and the possibility was all birthed and made possible by that word maybe. This episode is about how you can make your running better by incorporating that word... maybe.. into your training and your running and your living, Thank you for listening!

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

This episode is about how you can make your running better by incorporating that word... maybe.. into your training and your running and your living, Thank you for listening!

item.description:
Episode 128. Winter Is Coming. Get Ready To Run It. 

Winter is coming. Just ask Jon Snow or really anyone from the Stark family. All my Game of Throne nerds are nodding their heads. The rest of you are confused. That's okay! Because this episode is actually about running. Running in winter to be specific. I start by telling you how to get into the groove. Yes, there will be a special nod to Madonna. And I'll talk about the importance of getting into gear and getting inspired and getting intelligent and getting inside and getting interesting and a whole lot more. So, get cozy. Grab a nice hot cup of something something. Maybe snuggle up with a blanket or quilt (shout out to Coach Tammie). Maybe put on some winter running apparel and hit the trails. It's up to you. Just make sure you hit play and listen to the podcast because it's a good one and it'll help you run a better winter. 

Cheers,

Coach Bennett


I know you all wanted Coach Tammie to host another episode. I get it. She rocks. But you do know she has her very own podcast, right? You can listen to it here. 

And be sure to check out Madonna rock Into The Groove at Live Aid here.

item.description:
Episode 127. Now What? 

The race is over. The training cycle is finished. You crossed. the finish line. Now what? I've wanted to do this episode for a few weeks. So, I was super excited when it came time to record it. The problem was that I had a bad cough. The kind of cough that won't let you speak more than two sentences without coughing. And, I'm in a car driving across the country. Luckily, I happen to know the best coach in the world AND I'm driving across the country with her. So, problem solved. You get the first GUEST HOST of Coach Bennett's Podcast - the one and only Tammie Bennett of the Show Up Society. Tammie takes over the show. And I just drove and tried not to cough the entire time. Yes, she recorded the entire podcast from the passengers seat of our car while we drove through Iowa. Tammie kept saying Nebraska. I had burned through the Cornhusker state before we started recording. But enough of my driving prowess. The real winners are all of you listeners because Coach Tammie is going to talk to you about how best to handle that time AFTER the big race, presentation, event, day, moment. Enjoy the show!

Cheers and thank you for listening,

Coach Bennett

Make sure you follow tammie and. check out all that she has to offer. She's a stone cold rock star and if you're looking for someone to help you get out of your own way... she's the one. You can check out the Show Up Society here. 

And be sure to give tammie's podcast a follow and a listen here!

But you'll need the strength and confidence to wait a minute and reset or refresh or retry or redo. I did just that today. Because I needed to tell myself to just wait a minute and take that minute to recharge. So, take 30 minutes here and now and listen to the latest episode of Coach Bennett's Podcast before you head out for a recovery run or long run or fartlek run or decide to start run...
item.description:
Episode 126. Just Wait A Minute

Sometimes all it takes to set a day straight or a run right is a minute. But you'll need the strength and confidence to wait a minute and reset or refresh or retry or redo. I did just that today. Because I needed to tell myself to just wait a minute and take that minute to recharge. So, take 30 minutes here and now and listen to the latest episode of Coach Bennett's Podcast before you head out for a recovery run or long run or fartlek run or decide to start running again after a race or an injury or a training cycle.  

Cheers and thank you for listening!

Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 125. How To Have A Better Race 

This is a special one. Because it's episode 125! And because running a race is special. And because we have Coach Bennett the Greater as a guest. That's right! Tammie Bennett of the Show Up Society is back! And this is also a special episode because you all asked for an episode on what you could do before race day and on the starting line on race day and on the other side of that starting line on race day so you have a better chance to run a better race. Well, you asked and this episode delivers. And yes, this episode is about running and this episode is not about running. Thank you so much to Tammie and thank you so much to all of you for listening and sharing this episode and this sport with so many. 

I appreciate you all. 

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

And be sure to check out tammie bennett on Instagram 

And check out the Show Up Society website

And also check out Sarah Hughes give a performance for the ages at the the Winter Olympics here.

This is a special one. Because it's episode 125! And because running a race is special. And because we have Coach Bennett the Greater as a guest. That's right! Tammie Bennett of the Show Up Society is back! And this is also a special episode because you all asked for an episode on what you could do before race day and on the starting line on race day and on the other side of that starting line on race day so you have a better chance to run a better race. Well, you asked and this episode delivers. And yes, this episode is about running and this episode is not about running. Thank you so much to Tammie and thank you so much to all of you for listening and sharing this episode and this sport with so many. 

I appreciate you all. 

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

And be sure to check out tammie bennett on Instagram 

And check out the Show Up Society website

And also check out Sarah Hughes give a performance for the ages at the the Winter Olympics here.

item.itunes:title: Moving On
item.title: Moving On
item.itunes:summary: Episode 124. Moving On Sometimes the hardest part of a running a race isn't the race. And sometimes it's not even the training you need to do so you can run race that race. Sometimes the hardest part is after the race. It's letting go. It's moving on. We can give so much to a training cycle. We can put so much into the journey. We can invest so much into race day and the outcome. We can suddenly find ourselves lost and adrift when it's all over. This episode is about acknowledging all of this...
item.description:
Episode 124. Moving On

Sometimes the hardest part of a running a race isn't the race. And sometimes it's not even the training you need to do so you can run race that race. Sometimes the hardest part is after the race. It's letting go. It's moving on. We can give so much to a training cycle. We can put so much into the journey. We can invest so much into race day and the outcome. We can suddenly find ourselves lost and adrift when it's all over. This episode is about acknowledging all of this. And it's about how you can handle the process of moving on so you can move forward. Yes, this is about running. And this is not about running. 

Cheers and thank you for listening!

Coach Bennett

So many questions. Even more answers in this episode. So, check it out. And thank you for inspiring me with your efforts ...
item.description:
Episode 123. Success

Success. What is it? How do you define it? How do you measure it? What happens when you don't reach a goal? Is there such a thing as partial success? So many questions. Even more answers in this episode. Inspired by getting to talk to so many of you at the Berlin Marathon, Portland Marathon, Chicago Marathon, I wanted to make sure we talked about success from the point of view of the athletes and a coach. So, check it out. And thank you for inspiring me with your efforts and attitudes and running. 

Cheers and thank you for listening!

Coach Bennett

This episode is inspired by so many of you that have reached out over the last few weeks to ask about the starting line. item.description:
Episode 122. On The Starting Line

We all stand there on starting lines. Together. Alone. With the unknown ahead of us. No guarantees. This episode is inspired by so many of you that have reached out over the last few weeks to ask about the starting line. Over the last few weeks I've been to the Berlin Marathon, the Portland Marathon, Half Marathon, 10K and Kids Mile, and now I'm recording this episode in Chicago with this great city's marathon only a couple of days away. I talk nerves, confidence, looking ahead and behind, what to focus on and what not to focus on and I talk about hope. I hope you enjoy the episode and I salute all you rock stars finding and crossing those starting lines in your life. 

Cheers and thank you for listening!

Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 121. Ready, Set, Go! 

This is a really special episode. Because I get to talk about something that is near and dear to my heart - how to introduce the sport to kids (and adults). About ten years ago, I helped create something called Ready, Set, Go! for Nike Running. It was a guide for both kids and the adult in their lives that was going to help introduce them to this wonderful sport. Well, we decided to refresh Ready, Set, Go! and I couldn't be more excited about it. 

So, I figured, why not make a podcast that goes over some of the concepts of the guide. And the best part is that the concepts for kids are basically the same concepts for adults when it comes to introducing someone that is new to the sport or starting over in the sport. If there are kids in your life that you believe would love the sport or you're a parent or you're thinking about becoming a coach or already are one then this episode is for you. 

And be sure to check out the refreshed Ready, Set, Go! guide here. 

Cheers and thank you for listening to the show and for sharing, celebrating and saving this fantastic sport that we've got. 

Coach Bennett

item.content:encoded:
Episode 121. Ready, Set, Go! 

This is a really special episode. Because I get to talk about something that is near and dear to my heart - how to introduce the sport to kids (and adults). About ten years ago, I helped create something called Ready, Set, Go! for Nike Running. It was a guide for both kids and the adult in their lives that was going to help introduce them to this wonderful sport. Well, we decided to refresh Ready, Set, Go! and I couldn't be more excited about it. 

So, I figured, why not make a podcast that goes over some of the concepts of the guide. And the best part is that the concepts for kids are basically the same concepts for adults when it comes to introducing someone that is new to the sport or starting over in the sport. If there are kids in your life that you believe would love the sport or you're a parent or you're thinking about becoming a coach or already are one then this episode is for you. 

And be sure to check out the refreshed Ready, Set, Go! guide here. 

Cheers and thank you for listening to the show and for sharing, celebrating and saving this fantastic sport that we've got. 

Coach Bennett

Episode 119. Getting Ready For The Big Day

You got a big day coming up? Is it a race? 5K? 10K? Marathon? Maybe it's an exam you need to take or a presentation you need to give or a conversation you need to have. No matter what your big day is you can be better prepared to take it on AND enjoy it. How? How can you be better prepared and enjoy that big day more? So happy you asked! Just listen to this episode and there's a real good chance you're going to come up big on the big day. 

Cheers and thank you for listening!

item.description:
Episode 117. All I Got

It's been a tough couple of weeks. So much travel. Dropping kids off on the other side of the country. ER visits. Sickness. Heimlich Maneuvers. 7 year streaks being broken. Podcasts not getting published... or at least getting published a week later. So, this is the episode you're getting. It won't be the longest Coach Bennett's Podcast ever. It won't have the most rambles or even gloriously terrible weak jokes. But it is all I got. And it's the best I could do. I hope it's enough for you. And I hope it helps. 

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 116: Goals: How To Set, Change, Drop and Achieve Them

It's always a great time to talk about goals. And when you talk about goals you need to talk about more than how to set them. You need to know how to change and adjust goals and how to drop goals too. Big goals. Little goals. Medium sized goals. Long term goals. Short term goals. We cover it all. So, enjoy the show and please know that one of my goals in making this episode was to help you have a better chance achieving all of your goals for having listened to it. 

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

item.itunes:summary: Episode 115. Get Out Of Your Own Way We all do it. We self-sabotage. We trip ourselves up. We set ourselves up for something less than success. Well, this episode is all about ways we can get out of our own ways so we can run ourselves somewhere better without the self-destructive hurdles and barriers we place in front of ourselves. And obviously you need to listen to Go Your Own Way by Fleetwood Mac. And because my brain decides to simply go rogue here is Don't Stop by Fleetwood Mac to...
item.description:
Episode 115. Get Out Of Your Own Way

We all do it. We self-sabotage. We trip ourselves up. We set ourselves up for something less than success. Well, this episode is all about ways we can get out of our own ways so we can run ourselves somewhere better without the self-destructive hurdles and barriers we place in front of ourselves. 

And obviously you need to listen to Go Your Own Way by Fleetwood Mac. And because my brain decides to simply go rogue here is Don't Stop by Fleetwood Mac too. Don't worry... after the episode you'll understand. 

And if you want to check out the glorious dive bar that also has the worlds best fried chicken you need to check out 'REEL M'INN TAVERN 

Cheers and thank you for listening to the show!

Coach Bennett

item.itunes:title: Recovery On The Run
item.title: Recovery On The Run
item.itunes:summary: Episode 114. Recovery On The Run You've probably heard me say that great running dependent on great recovery. Well, it's the truth. And if you can recover great and do that consistently you're going to give yourself the best chance to run great. So, listen up. Because this episode is all about how you can recover better and recover better consistently. None of the things I talk about in this episode are recovery techniques that you can't start taking advantage of today. Because almost everyth...
item.description:
Episode 114. Recovery On The Run

You've probably heard me say that great running dependent on great recovery. Well, it's the truth. And if you can recover great and do that consistently you're going to give yourself the best chance to run great. So, listen up. Because this episode is all about how you can recover better and recover better consistently. None of the things I talk about in this episode are recovery techniques that you can't start taking advantage of today. Because almost everything I talk about in this episode you are probably already doing! I'm just going to tell you how you can do it all a little better. And if you can recover a little better consistently and in a whole bunch of different ways... well, you're going to experiencing some great recovery. And you know what depends on great recovery? 

Cheers and thank you for listening!

Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 113. Get Fast

Buckle up buttercup! Because we're about to go fast talking about getting fast. I do my best to rip through this episode... fast. That means the rambling on this episode may not be non-existent but it's sure not excessive. And yes, it was not easy for me to not ramble. It's also not easy for me to not write sentences with double negatives. I digress. Sit back or start it up... no matter how your listening to the show or what you're doing while you listen to the show... I hope you enjoy the episode. And I hope you end the episode knowing a few ways you can get faster. 

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

item.title: 5 Ways To Build Confidence
item.itunes:summary: Episode 112. 5 Ways To Build Confidence Being confident takes work. And that means that confidence is something that can be built up, grown, maintained. That also means that confidence is something that can be torn down, weakened, atrophy. So, we're going to discuss a few ways that you can work on and build confidence. I know I named this episode 5 Ways To Build Confidence but I'm not entirely sure how many ways I actually talk about. I feel like it's more than 5 though. Anyway, it's a great ...
item.description:
Episode 112. 5 Ways To Build Confidence

Being confident takes work. And that means that confidence is something that can be built up, grown, maintained. That also means that confidence is something that can be torn down, weakened, atrophy. So, we're going to discuss a few ways that you can work on and build confidence. I know I named this episode 5 Ways To Build Confidence but I'm not entirely sure how many ways I actually talk about. I feel like it's more than 5 though. Anyway, it's a great episode and you're a great person so it seems like you and the episode should get together. I'm confident you're going to enjoy it. 

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

Episode 112. 5 Ways To Build Confidence

Being confident takes work. And that means that confidence is something that can be built up, grown, maintained. That also means that confidence is something that can be torn down, weakened, atrophy. item.description:
Episode 111. Setbacks (How To Deal With Them)

It's inevitable. If you show up consistently... if you chase after great goals... if you get better and want to get better... if you try your best... you're going to need to deal with setbacks. How you deal with those setbacks will help decide just how consistent you'll continue to be and whether you'll chase down those goals or not and whether you'll get better and better and if you'll get closer to your best. So, let's talk setbacks. Shall we? We shall! 

Thank you for listening to the show!

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

Episode 111. Setbacks (How To Deal With Them)

It's inevitable. If you show up consistently... if you chase after great goals... if you get better and want to get better... if you try your best... you're going to need to deal with setbacks. How you deal with those setbacks will help decide just how consistent you'll continue to be and whether you'll chase down those goals or not and whether you'll get better and better and if you'll get closer to your best. So, let's talk setbacks. Shall we? We shall! 

Thank you for listening to the show!

Cheers,

item.title: Risk and Reward
item.itunes:summary: Episode 110. Risk and Reward. Some risks can lead to great rewards. Some rewards are not worth the risks. Whether you realize it or not every run and race you do and rest day you take has risks and rewards associated with them. This episode is all about the relationship between the two. This episode was inspired by the mile run by Faith Kipyegon in her Breaking 4 attempt and the 1500m race run by Jessica Hull at the Prefontaine Classic the following week. Enjoy the episode and be sure to chec...
item.description:
Episode 110. Risk and Reward.

Some risks can lead to great rewards. Some rewards are not worth the risks. Whether you realize it or not every run and race you do and rest day you take has risks and rewards associated with them. This episode is all about the relationship between the two. This episode was inspired by the mile run by Faith Kipyegon in her Breaking 4 attempt and the 1500m race run by Jessica Hull at the Prefontaine Classic the following week. Enjoy the episode and be sure to check out the links to both those races in the show notes below. 

Cheers and thank you for listening. 

Coach Bennett

Watch the women's 1500m race at the 2025 Prefontaine Classic 

Watch the Breaking4 Mile with Faith Kipyegon

item.itunes:title: It's OK To Not Be OK
item.title: It's OK To Not Be OK
item.itunes:summary: Episode 109. It's OK To Not Be OK It's OK to be OK. It's OK to not be OK. But it's not OK to say you're OK when you're not OK. And it sounds like a lot of you are not OK. The good thing is that you are reaching out to me and admitting that you're not OK. That takes honesty and strength. Both of those things help you when it comes to running by the way. You're also reaching out and asking for some help. (Asking for help is another sign or strength.) That's why I'm doing this episode. And if yo...
item.description:
Episode 109. It's OK To Not Be OK

It's OK to be OK. It's OK to not be OK. But it's not OK to say you're OK when you're not OK. And it sounds like a lot of you are not OK. The good thing is that you are reaching out to me and admitting that you're not OK. That takes honesty and strength. Both of those things help you when it comes to running by the way. You're also reaching out and asking for some help. (Asking for help is another sign or strength.)

That's why I'm doing this episode. And if you are OK... this episode is for you too. Because even though this time you are OK there will be some times when you are not OK. So, this episode will help for those times. OK? 

Let me tell you this, too. If you can deal with negative (and positive) stressors in your life better then you will run better. More importantly, you'll live better. And if you were wondering... yes, this episode is about running. And this episode is not about running. 

Cheers and thank you for listening. 

CB

item.content:encoded:
Episode 109. It's OK To Not Be OK

It's OK to be OK. It's OK to not be OK. But it's not OK to say you're OK when you're not OK. And it sounds like a lot of you are not OK. The good thing is that you are reaching out to me and admitting that you're not OK. That takes honesty and strength. Both of those things help you when it comes to running by the way. You're also reaching out and asking for some help. (Asking for help is another sign or strength.)

That's why I'm doing this episode. And if you are OK... this episode is for you too. Because even though this time you are OK there will be some times when you are not OK. So, this episode will help for those times. OK? 

Let me tell you this, too. If you can deal with negative (and positive) stressors in your life better then you will run better. More importantly, you'll live better. And if you were wondering... yes, this episode is about running. And this episode is not about running. 

Cheers and thank you for listening.

item.itunes:title: How To Slow Down (So You Can Speed Up)
item.title: How To Slow Down (So You Can Speed Up)
item.itunes:summary: Episode 108. How To Slow Down (So You Can Speed Up) Sometimes you need to slow down so you can speed up. Yes, it makes sense. And it's true. In fact, it's essential to understand if you want to get better at this fantastic sport. That's why we're going to talk about it on this episode. You'll learn why it's important and how to do it just by listening to the show. Pretty good deal if you ask me. So, sit back or stand up or go for a walk or a run and enjoy the podcast. And yes, this episode is...
item.description:
Episode 108. How To Slow Down (So You Can Speed Up)

Sometimes you need to slow down so you can speed up. Yes, it makes sense. And it's true. In fact, it's essential to understand if you want to get better at this fantastic sport. That's why we're going to talk about it on this episode. You'll learn why it's important and how to do it just by listening to the show. Pretty good deal if you ask me. So, sit back or stand up or go for a walk or a run and enjoy the podcast. And yes, this episode is about running and this is not about running.  Thank you for listening!

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

item.itunes:title: 7 Ways To Make Your Running More Fun
item.title: 7 Ways To Make Your Running More Fun
item.itunes:summary: Episode 107. 7 Ways To Make Your Running More Fun It doesn't matter how fast or how far you can run. There's always an opportunity to make your running more fun. And if you can make your running more fun then you can make your running better and if you can make your running better then you can make the runner doing that running better. That was definitely a run on sentence. But I hope it was easy to understand. Because if you can figure out how to get better and have fun getting better then y...
item.description:
Episode 107. 7 Ways To Make Your Running More Fun

It doesn't matter how fast or how far you can run. There's always an opportunity to make your running more fun. And if you can make your running more fun then you can make your running better and if you can make your running better then you can make the runner doing that running better. That was definitely a run on sentence. But I hope it was easy to understand. Because if you can figure out how to get better and have fun getting better then you can get better... and better... and better. 

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 106.  I Believe. 

I figured it was time that I told you what I believe in. Especially, since I believe in you! But this is episode goes old school. There was once a time when seven statements I wrote on a cheap legal pad became the foundation for NRC... and how so may of us (including me) look at ourselves and support ourselves and cheer for ourselves. YOU deserve the best if you are trying your best.

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

item.content:encoded:
Episode 106.  I Believe. 

I figured it was time that I told you what I believe in. Especially, since I believe in you! But this is episode goes old school. There was once a time when seven statements I wrote on a cheap legal pad became the foundation for NRC... and how so may of us (including me) look at ourselves and support ourselves and cheer for ourselves. YOU deserve the best if you are trying your best.

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 105.  Happy Global Running Day

Sure, this episode may be dropping the day after Global Running Day in many parts of the world... the sentiment remains true. Happy Global Running Day! I'm taking the opportunity to state what it means to be a runner and how we can best acknowledge and experience and what it means to be a part of this sport. So, press play and turn up the volume and get ready to celebrate, share and save the sport on Global Running Day 2025... or the day after Global Running Day 2025... or really whatever day you are actually listening to this episode. 

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

Episode 104.  7 Ways To Make Your Summer Running Better

This could actually be 5 or 6 or maybe even 8 or 9 ways. I'm not exactly sure. I kind of went into a zone there for a little while and when I came out of it the episode was done. Doesn't really matter. What does matter is that by the end of the episode you're going to know a whole bunch of different ways you can make your summer running better! And that's a good thing! So, enjoy the episode and thank you for listening. 

Cheers,

item.itunes:title: Comebacks
item.title: Comebacks
item.itunes:summary: Episode 103. Comebacks Don't call it a comeback? No, call it a comeback! Coming back from an injury? It's a comeback. Coming back from sickness? It's a comeback. Coming back from a life event? It's a comeback. Coming back from a long season or a big setback or a tough race or a great one? Comebacks! You know what? Every time you come back to a starting line it's a comeback! This episode is all about how you can make any comeback a return to the starting line that gets you to a finish line exc...
item.description:
Episode 103. Comebacks

Don't call it a comeback? No, call it a comeback! Coming back from an injury? It's a comeback. Coming back from sickness? It's a comeback. Coming back from a life event? It's a comeback. Coming back from a long season or a big setback or a tough race or a great one? Comebacks! You know what? Every time you come back to a starting line it's a comeback! This episode is all about how you can make any comeback a return to the starting line that gets you to a finish line excited about the next starting line. And yes, this is an episode that I think you'll come back to. 

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.content:encoded:
Episode 103. Comebacks

Don't call it a comeback? No, call it a comeback! Coming back from an injury? It's a comeback. Coming back from sickness? It's a comeback. Coming back from a life event? It's a comeback. Coming back from a long season or a big setback or a tough race or a great one? Comebacks! You know what? Every time you come back to a starting line it's a comeback! This episode is all about how you can make any comeback a return to the starting line that gets you to a finish line excited about the next starting line. And yes, this is an episode that I think you'll come back to. 

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.title: How To Do More
item.itunes:summary: Episode 102. How To Do More So, you want to know how to do more? I get it. More is better, right? Well, maybe. Maybe not. That's why before we decide what we need to do more of (or less of) we need to have a conversation. And that's what this episode is... a conversation. I'll give you a series of questions that you need to ask and answer before you decide what you need to do more of. If you're an athlete then this is an essential episode. And if you have a body you're an athlete. This ...
item.description:
Episode 102. How To Do More

So, you want to know how to do more? I get it. More is better, right? Well, maybe. Maybe not. That's why before we decide what we need to do more of (or less of) we need to have a conversation. And that's what this episode is... a conversation. I'll give you a series of questions that you need to ask and answer  before you decide what you need to do more of. If you're an athlete then this is an essential episode. And if you have a body you're an athlete. This is also an essential episode for all you coaches out there. And if you have a heart then you are a coach. So, this is an episode for everyone. Yes, that mean you.

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.content:encoded:
Episode 102. How To Do More

So, you want to know how to do more? I get it. More is better, right? Well, maybe. Maybe not. That's why before we decide what we need to do more of (or less of) we need to have a conversation. And that's what this episode is... a conversation. I'll give you a series of questions that you need to ask and answer  before you decide what you need to do more of. If you're an athlete then this is an essential episode. And if you have a body you're an athlete. This is also an essential episode for all you coaches out there. And if you have a heart then you are a coach. So, this is an episode for everyone. Yes, that mean you.

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.itunes:title: Searching For Balance
item.title: Searching For Balance
item.itunes:summary: Episode 101. Searching For Balance. We're all searching for some balance in our lives and in our running. But I don't think there's such a thing as perfect balance. I believe we're just trying to balance all our imbalances as best as we can. And that's what this episode is all about. If we can look from a different and more supportive point of view and give a little more grace and have a little more empathy towards out own balancing acts we're going to have a better chance of maintainin...
item.description:
Episode 101. Searching For Balance. 

We're all searching for some balance in our lives and in our running. But I don't think there's such a thing as perfect balance. I believe we're just trying to balance all our imbalances as best as we can. And that's what this episode is all about. If we can look from a different and more supportive point of view and give a little more grace and have a little more empathy towards out own balancing acts we're going to have a better chance of maintaining that balance. So, enjoy the podcast and get ready for some unicycle talk. Pretty sure it's the first time I've talked about a unicycle. 

Cheers and thank you for listening to the show!

Coach Bennett

item.content:encoded:
Episode 101. Searching For Balance. 

We're all searching for some balance in our lives and in our running. But I don't think there's such a thing as perfect balance. I believe we're just trying to balance all our imbalances as best as we can. And that's what this episode is all about. If we can look from a different and more supportive point of view and give a little more grace and have a little more empathy towards out own balancing acts we're going to have a better chance of maintaining that balance. So, enjoy the podcast and get ready for some unicycle talk. Pretty sure it's the first time I've talked about a unicycle. 

Cheers and thank you for listening to the show!

Coach Bennett

Episode 100.  Why Do You Run? 

Why do you run? This is a conversation... but it's also a celebration of running. And it's a celebration of runners. That means it's a celebration of YOU! Hey, great opportunity to thank you for getting us here... Episode 100. This podcast is for YOU! So, enjoy the show. Be ready to join in on the discussion. Because this is about why WE run. And there is no WE without YOU. 

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

And there is no WE without YOU.

I said that Faith Kipyegon has committed to trying to run under 4 minutes for a mile in June. You read that right, twice. item.description:
Episode 99. You Gotta Have Faith 

It was announced today that Faith Kipyegon is going to try and break 4 in the Mile this Summer in Paris. What was that? I said that Faith Kipyegon has committed to trying to run under 4 minutes for a mile in June. You read that right, twice. 

This episode is about Faith and faith and Breaking 4 (that's what this is being called) and Breaking 2 and Eliud Kipchoge and this episode is about you and me and goals and confidence and failure and success and courage and Prince and George Michael. It's a great episode. Do you believe me? I guess you just need to have some faith. 

Cheers ank thank you for listening to the show!

Coach Bennett

Here are some videos to go along with the links above. 


Watch Faith Kipyegon set the WR in the Mile

Episode 98. A Pep Talk For You (And Me)

This wasn't what I had originally planned for this episode. But sometimes the best plan is a different plan than what was originally planned. So, I listened to all of you and recorded the episode you all needed to hear and not the episode I wanted to make. I hope you enjoy this little pep talk. And to be honest... I think I needed it just as much if not more than any of you. 

Thank you for listening to and sharing Coach Bennett's Podcast.

Cheers,

item.title: How To Schedule Your Runs
item.itunes:summary: Episode 97. How To Schedule Your Runs Sometimes the hardest part of running isn't the running. It's scheduling the running. When and why do we run when we run? Is it okay to switch up the schedule? How flexible can we be? What should we take into consideration when setting up a week or weeks of training? Wouldn't it be great if there was somewhere to get all these questions answered? I'm wicked witty. See what I did there? Because here is a place where you can get all these questions an...
item.description:
Episode 97.  How To Schedule Your Runs

Sometimes the hardest part of running isn't the running. It's scheduling the running. When and why do we run when we run? Is it okay to switch up the schedule? How flexible can we be? What should we take into consideration when setting up a week or weeks of training? Wouldn't it be great if there was somewhere to get all these questions answered? I'm wicked witty. See what I did there? Because here is a place where you can get all these questions answered. And that place is wherever you are right now. All you need to do is press play on episode 97. 

Cheers and thank you for listening!

Coach Bennett

Episode 97.  How To Schedule Your Runs

Sometimes the hardest part of running isn't the running. It's scheduling the running. When and why do we run when we run? Is it okay to switch up the schedule? How flexible can we be? See what I did there? Because here is a place where you can get all these questions answered. And that place is wherever you are right now. All you need to do is press play on episode 97. 

Cheers and thank you for listening!

item.description:
Episode 96. How To Get Through A Tough Run 

It doesn't matter how fast you are. It doesn't mater how long you've been running, It doesn't matter how experienced you are as a runner. It doesn't matter how many times you've seen the Gaslight Anthem in concert. You're going to have some tough runs. I'm hoping that this episode helps. Because if you can have a better run when you you're having a tough run you';re going to have better runs and that means you will become a better runner.

Enjoy the episode and thank you for listening to the podcast!

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

A link you may enjoy...

Watch the 2000 Olympic Men's 10K. It's one of the best races of all time.

item.itunes:summary: Episode 95. How To Make Gains With No Pains Anyone that’s says “no pain no gain” has no idea what they’re talking about. There are so many gains you can make with no pain involved. Doesn’t mean there won’t be hard runs and tough races and struggles along the way on your running journey. But none of that should be dependent on pain. Give this episode a listen and if you’ve still got that poster from the 80’s or 90’s up on your wall that say “No Pain. No Gain”… it’s time to take it down. ...
item.description:
Episode 95. How To Make Gains With No Pains

Anyone that’s says “no pain no gain” has no idea what they’re talking about. There are so many gains you can make with no pain involved. Doesn’t mean there won’t be hard runs and tough races and struggles along the way on your running journey. But none of that should be dependent on pain. Give this episode a listen and if you’ve still got that poster from the 80’s or 90’s up on your wall that say “No Pain. No Gain”… it’s time to take it down. 

Cheers and thank you for listening to the show. 

Coach Bennett

item.content:encoded:
Episode 95. How To Make Gains With No Pains

Anyone that’s says “no pain no gain” has no idea what they’re talking about. There are so many gains you can make with no pain involved. Doesn’t mean there won’t be hard runs and tough races and struggles along the way on your running journey. But none of that should be dependent on pain. Give this episode a listen and if you’ve still got that poster from the 80’s or 90’s up on your wall that say “No Pain. No Gain”… it’s time to take it down. 

Cheers and thank you for listening to the show. 

Coach Bennett

That's just being obnoxious. item.description:
Episode 94. 5 Tips For A Better Long Run. 

Why give you one tip for a better long run when I can give you five? No need ot ask why not I don't give six or seven. That's just being obnoxious. Give this oddly absent of rambles episode a listen and learn about how being fueled and hydrated, knowing where you are going and what you are doing, packing a parachute and one or two other tips I can't remember right now can help you have the best possible long run.

 Cheers to you and thank you for running and listening to the podcast!

Coach Bennett

item.itunes:title: The Story Of The First Gold In The Women’s Marathon
item.title: The Story Of The First Gold In The Women’s Marathon
item.itunes:summary: Episode 93. The Story Of The First Gold In The Women’s Marathon It's the first race I can remember watching. And it's stayed with me ever since. I'm talking about the 1984 Women's Olympic Marathon. This was the first time women competed in the Marathon in the Olympic Games. I was just a little kid. But I knew that what I was watching was special. From the breakaway move Joan Benoit (Samuelson) made barely 5K into the race to the final brutal lap of Gabriela Andersen-Scheiss this race ma...
item.description:
Episode 93. The Story Of The First Gold In The Women’s Marathon 

It's the first race I can remember watching. And it's stayed with me ever since. I'm talking about the 1984 Women's Olympic Marathon. This was the first time women competed in the Marathon in the Olympic Games. I was just a little kid. But I knew that what I was watching was special. From the breakaway move Joan Benoit (Samuelson) made barely 5K into the race to the final brutal lap of Gabriela Andersen-Scheiss this race made such impact on an incredibly cute little boy from New Jersey that he eventually would grow up (still incredibly cute) and dedicate most of his life to the sport of running. This is story time. 

Thank you for listening and I hope you enjoy the episode. 

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

A few links you may enjoy...

Watch the video I made about Joanie and her Olympic win here.

Here's another video I made a long time ago. It starts with Gabriela Andersen-Scheiss. And yes, I know the video quality is less than desired. Did I mention I made it a long time ago? 

Here's a short video on Gabriela Andersen-Sheiss and her 1984 Olympic marathon finish.

It's the first race I can remember watching. And it's stayed with me ever since. I'm talking about the 1984 Women's Olympic Marathon. From the breakaway move Joan Benoit (Samuelson) made barely 5K into the race to the final brutal lap of Gabriela Andersen-Scheiss this race made such impact on an incredibly cute little boy from New Jersey that he eventually would grow up (still incredibly cute) and dedicate most of his life to the sport of running. This is story time. 

Thank you for listening and I hope you enjoy the episode. 

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.title: You And The Best Runners In The World
item.itunes:summary: Episode 92. You And The Best Runners In The World. You got a lot in common with the best runners in the world. You know that right? Because you should know that. In fact, you have way more in common than not. Don't believe me? That's fine. You once believed you couldn't do all sorts of things that you've since done. So, I'm used to it. I'm hoping that this episode goes a long way to remedying your disbelief. And if you can start to understand just how much you and the best runners in the worl...
item.description:
Episode 92. You And The Best Runners In The World.

You got a lot in common with the best runners in the world. You know that right? Because you should know that. In fact, you have way more in common than not. Don't believe me? That's fine. You once believed you couldn't do all sorts of things that you've since done. So, I'm used to it. I'm hoping that this episode goes a long way to remedying your disbelief. And if you can start to understand just how much you and the best runners in the world have in common you may just start to look at what you do in a new way... a way that affirms just how extraordinary it is to find and cross all those starting line sin our lives. 

Enjoy the episode. 

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

Episode 92. You And The Best Runners In The World.

You got a lot in common with the best runners in the world. You know that right? Because you should know that. In fact, you have way more in common than not. Don't believe me? That's fine. You once believed you couldn't do all sorts of things that you've since done. So, I'm used to it. I'm hoping that this episode goes a long way to remedying your disbelief. And if you can start to understand just how much you and the best runners in the world have in common you may just start to look at what you do in a new way... a way that affirms just how extraordinary it is to find and cross all those starting line sin our lives. 

Enjoy the episode.

item.itunes:title: How To Coach Yourself (And Others) Better
item.title: How To Coach Yourself (And Others) Better
item.itunes:summary: Episode 91. How To Coach Yourself (And Others) Better One of the goals I have as a coach is that you need me less and less. That's right. Every time we get together I want you to be closer to becoming your own best coach. And we're going to discuss some of the way you can work on becoming a better coach to yourself right now. Spoiler alert: it's going to be a series of questions to ask yourself. And the key to answering those questions the right way is to be honest because an honest answer is...
item.description:
Episode 91. How To Coach Yourself (And Others) Better

One of the goals I have as a coach is that you need me less and less. That's right. Every time we get together I want you to be closer to becoming your own best coach. And we're going to discuss some of the way you can work on becoming a better coach to yourself right now. Spoiler alert: it's going to be a series of questions to ask yourself. And the key to answering those questions the right way is to be honest because an honest answer is always a right answer. So, get ready to ask yourself questions about your purposes for running, your strengths and weaknesses, your commitment level, and so much more. Remember, our time together is about you getting to the point where you demote me from and elevate you to head coach. This episode gets you closer. 

Thank you for listening!

Coach Bennett

Send us a message

item.description:
Episode 90: How To Run Better In Cold Weather

If you're in the depths of winter or winter is on its way or you're just one of those fantastic souls that's always game for learning more... this episode is for you. I'll take you through some of the most foundational ways to make your running better on those cold, cold runs. We talk about windchill and base layers and hydration and sweat and hats with pom poms on them and so much more. So, what are you waiting for? Press start and listen to the latest episode of Coach Bennett's Podcast!

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

item.itunes:title: Stressed Out
item.title: Stressed Out
item.itunes:summary: Episode 89. Stressed Out Stress is real. Stress is a natural response we have to situations that feel out of control or hard to manage. So, if you're human... you're going to get stressed out. And right now, there seems to be a whole of stressed out runners. We're going to talk about what stress is. We're going to talk about how stress and especially long term and heightened stress can have negative impacts on your running. But this episode is about running and this is not about running...
item.description:
Episode 89. Stressed Out

Stress is real. Stress is a natural response we have to situations that feel out of control or hard to manage. So, if you're human... you're going to get stressed out. And right now, there seems to be a whole of stressed out runners. 

We're going to talk about what stress is. We're going to talk about how stress and especially long term and heightened stress can have negative impacts on your running. But this episode is about running and this is not about running. Because stress does not only hurt your running. Stress can hurt your life before the starting line and after the finish line is crossed. And you spend a whole lot more time living that life beyond those lines than running those meters, minutes and miles between them. So, if we can learn how to handle or deal with our stress better we can run and live better. 

And if we need help... help that goes beyond what we talk about in this podcast we know that asking for help from experts is not a sign of weakness. Rather, it's a sign of badass strength. 

Hope this episode helps. 

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.itunes:title: All The Small Things
item.title: All The Small Things
item.itunes:summary: Episode 88. All The Small Things Yes, it's a Blink 182 song. And yes, it's a great song. But this episode may be even better. Because we're talking about all the small things that you can do to make those big things you want to do easier to do... or at least more likely to be done by you. Now seems like a great time for this episode because now is always the right time ot talk about this topic. And because everyone seems to be stressed out and exhausted and having hard time making it th...
item.description:
Episode 88.  All The Small Things

Yes, it's a Blink 182 song. And yes, it's a great song. But this episode may be even better. Because we're talking about all the small things that you can do to make those big things you want to do easier to do... or at least more likely to be done by you. Now seems like a great time for this episode because now is always the right time ot talk about this topic. And because everyone seems to be stressed out and exhausted and having hard time making it the starting lines in their lives that they want to get to and over. The episode goes over small things like planning ahead, the W's, the wholly triumvirate of eating and drinking and sleeping, raditudes and baditudes, and there's even a ramble about just how great 1989 was for albums. Now you may be wondering why I go one and on about albums from 1989. That's because I mistakenly believed this was episode 89. It's not. It's episode 88. 

Hope you enjoy the episode and more importantly I hope it helps. 

Thank you for listening to the show. 

Cheers,

Coach Bennett

A few links for you...

All The Small Things music video

Little Things music video

One kinda sorta random list of the best albums of 1989 that I don't totally agree with but at least it has most of the top albums listed. Anyway, it does a good enough job. You'll get the idea. 

Send us a message

item.itunes:title: Metrics That Really Matter
item.title: Metrics That Really Matter
item.itunes:summary: Episode 87. Metrics That Really Matter You've got the classics. You know what I'm talking about. The OG running metrics. Minutes. Meters. Miles. But there are some new cats and dogs in town too. Metrics that can also help you measure and understand your performance and progression. Metrics like cadence and heart rate. And there are more like the wildly diverse personal bests metric and the always fun metric known as... fun. Yes, fun can and should be a metric you pay attention to when ...
item.description:
Episode 87.  Metrics That Really Matter

You've got the classics. You know what I'm talking about. The OG running metrics. Minutes. Meters. Miles. But there are some new cats and dogs in town too. Metrics that can also help you measure and understand your performance and progression. Metrics like cadence and heart rate. And there are more like the wildly diverse personal bests metric and the always fun metric known as... fun. Yes, fun can and should be a metric you pay attention to when it comes to your running. I hope you enjoy the discussion and that it helps you measure and understand you're running performance and progression a little bit more.

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

A few links for ya!

Sign up for BEST RUNNER EVER! We're opening our month long community running group February 1st. Get all the details and information on how to register by clicking on the link and then tapping on BRE in the menu on the Show Up Society website.

Watch this little video I made and learn how you can get better at running while you're not running here. In fact, you'll learn how to get better at running before you have that first cup of coffee in the morning.

Enjoy the fresh new updated Coach Bennett's Mixtape.

item.itunes:summary: Episode 86. Motivation. My motivation for this episode was you. Yes, you. You've been reaching out telling me you're losing motivation or you're afraid your motivation is lost. Well, if you're reaching out that means you still got some. And if you listen to this episode my hope is that you'l have a little more motivation to fuel what it is that you want and need to do. I could ramble on about all the different things that I go over in this episode but I think it's best if you just hit...
item.description:
Episode 86.  Motivation.

My motivation for this episode was you. Yes, you. You've been reaching out telling me you're losing motivation or you're afraid your motivation is lost. Well, if you're reaching out that means you still got some. And if you listen to this episode my hope is that you'l have a little more motivation to fuel what it is that you want and need to do. I could ramble on about all the different things that I go over in this episode but I think it's best if you just hit play and listen. Oh yeah, one more thing... you're not alone.

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

A few links for you:

Here's that Super Rad 1986 Playlist I said I might make. Well, I made it.

item.description:
Episode 85. Inspire.

Inspire. Inspiration. Inspired. That's a verb, noun and adjective there. All essential to a successful runner. And all three can seem elusive at times. This episode breaks down the meaning of those words. Then we discuss how and who we can inspire, what it means to be inspired, and where we can find inspiration. Theres even a mindset minute with coach bennett the greater, tammie bennett of the show up society. If all that talk about nouns and verbs and adjectives and definitions didn't inspire you already I know you're inspired now that coach tammie is here. I get it. She inspires me too. Enjoy the episode and thank you for listening to the podcast.

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

Here's a video I made a long time ago that I think you may find inspiring.

Send us a message

item.itunes:title: When Easy Isn't Easy
item.title: When Easy Isn't Easy
item.itunes:summary: Episode 84. When Easy Isn't Easy. Sometimes it can feel like everything is hard. Yes, sometimes it can feel like even easy isn't so easy. That's okay. Because on this episode we're going to talk about how we can better handle times like these. Sometimes the key is simply asking ourselves a few questions. Other times it's about making changes, giving ourselves a little grace, backing off or yes... pushing on. There are a lot of takeaways from this episode and it's one that makes sense ...
item.description:
Episode 84.  When Easy Isn't Easy.

Sometimes it can feel like everything is hard. Yes, sometimes it can feel like even easy isn't so easy. That's okay. Because on this episode we're going to talk about how we can better handle times like these. Sometimes the key is simply asking ourselves a few questions. Other times it's about making changes, giving ourselves a little grace, backing off or yes... pushing on. There are a lot of takeaways from this episode and it's one that makes sense to drop now considering the time of year. But this is a topic that is best when it's needed. And when it's needed doesn't need to have anything to do with the time of year.

Cheers and thank you for listening!
Coach Bennett

⭐️Here are a few links you might enjoy:

All the details about the BEST RUNNER EVER WINTER EDITION program

RollingStone Magazine article on 1984 being the best year ever for pop music

My epic 80's For The Win Mixtape on Spotify

item.content:encoded:
Episode 84.  When Easy Isn't Easy.

Sometimes it can feel like everything is hard. Yes, sometimes it can feel like even easy isn't so easy. That's okay. Because on this episode we're going to talk about how we can better handle times like these. Sometimes the key is simply asking ourselves a few questions. Other times it's about making changes, giving ourselves a little grace, backing off or yes... pushing on. There are a lot of takeaways from this episode and it's one that makes sense to drop now considering the time of year. But this is a topic that is best when it's needed. And when it's needed doesn't need to have anything to do with the time of year.

Cheers and thank you for listening!
Coach Bennett

⭐️Here are a few links you might enjoy:

All the details about the BEST RUNNER EVER WINTER EDITION program

RollingStone Magazine article on 1984 being the best year ever for pop music

My epic 80's For The Win Mixtape on Spotify

item.description:
Episode 83.  This Can Be Your Best Year Ever!

Welcome to 2025! Could this be your best... year... ever? Maybe. At least it makes sense to try to have your best year ever. It does to me anyway. And I hope after this episode it makes sense to you too. That's why we're going to go over some of the ways I think you can set yourself up to have your best year or training cycle or season of running ever. We'll talk about belief and pints of water and rocks in your backpack (just listen to the episode). And thank you for meeting me here on yet  another starting line. Now press start and let's get to the other side of that line! Because that's where extraordinary lives.

Cheers
Coach Bennett

item.itunes:title: New Year's Resolve
item.title: New Year's Resolve
item.itunes:summary: Episode 82. New Year's Resolve. I love resolutions. I do. But I love resolve even more. What can I say? I'm a verbs guy. Nouns and adjectives are wonderful. But if we are being honest then we know that it's the verbs that power and inspire all those nouns we love and adjectives we aim for. And that's why we're going to talk about resolutions through the lens of that word resolve. And we're going to talk about how we can set ourselves up to carry out those resolutions we are reso...
item.description:
Episode 82.  New Year's Resolve.

I love resolutions. I do. But I love resolve even more. What can I say? I'm a verbs guy. Nouns and adjectives are wonderful. But if we are being honest then we know that it's the verbs that power and inspire all those nouns we love and adjectives we aim for. And that's why we're going to talk about resolutions through the lens of that word resolve. And we're going to talk about how we can set ourselves up to carry out those resolutions we are resolving to do! Too often we set ourselves up for failure when we start a new year. And too often we diminish and denigrate ourselves when we set new year's resolutions. This episode is all about how we can set ourselves up for success by being in service of ourselves. We talk about purpose, consistency, self-coaching, motivation and so much more. So, get ready for a new year, new month, new day, new moment with this new episode of Coach Bennett's Podcast.

Cheers and thank you for listening!
Coach Bennett

item.itunes:summary: Episode 81. Strength It's not just about lifting weights. It's not just physical. It's not just physical and it's not just mental. It's more. So much more. And that means you can be strong in so many different ways. It means you can work on being strong in so many different ways. And it means that every run offers you the opportunity to become stronger. By the way, if you run... you are strong. If you run... you can be stronger. If you run... you need to listen to this episode. ...
item.description:
Episode 81.   Strength

It's not just about lifting weights. It's not just physical. It's not just physical and it's not just mental. It's more. So much more. And that means you can be strong in so many different ways. It means you can work on being strong in so many different ways. And it means that every run offers you the opportunity to become stronger. By the way, if you run... you are strong. If you run... you can be stronger. If you run... you need to listen to this episode.

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.title: How To Run More With Less Time
item.itunes:summary: Episode 80. How To Run More With Less Time Sometimes it feels like we've got no time to do the things that we love to spend our time doing. And when we stop doing those things that we want to do that recharge and refresh us we can struggle to find the energy and inspiration to do those things that we need to do. Well, this episode is all about how we can run more when we have less time. Seems like it makes no sense, right? Great! Because that means there is a better chance you are going to ...
item.description:
Episode 80.
How To Run More With Less Time

Sometimes it feels like we've got no time to do the things that we love to spend our time doing. And when we stop doing those things that we want to do that recharge and refresh us we can struggle to find the energy and inspiration to do those things that we need to do. Well, this episode is all about how we can run more when we have less time. Seems like it makes no sense, right? Great! Because that means there is a better chance you are going to listen to the episode. And bonus... there's even a Mindset Minutes. Yes, Minutes. The always awesome coach tammie of the show up society is back to lead you through a visualization exercise and it takes minutes. Episode 80 is a blast. And yes, I know that I screwed up and my mic was not attached to the computer so the audio is less than its usual stellar quality. But I make up for it with extraordinary content so it all evens out.

Cheers and thank you for listening!
Coach Bennett

item.title: How To Keep Going
item.itunes:summary: Episode 79. How To Keep Going Could be that time of year... could be because of work or school or because of obligations or responsibilities. Could be lack of motivation or inspiration. Could just be that it's hard to find the progress or earn a next win. Whatever the case may be it can be hard to keep going towards where or what you want to get to. Well, that's why we got this episode. Join Coach Bennett (that's me... and it's always weird writing in the third person) as he (again wit...
item.description:
Episode 79.  How To Keep Going

Could be that time of year... could be because of work or school or because of obligations or responsibilities. Could be lack of motivation or inspiration. Could just be that it's hard to find the progress or earn a next win. Whatever the case may be it can be hard to keep going towards where or what you want to get to. Well, that's why we got this episode. Join Coach Bennett (that's me... and it's always weird writing in the third person) as he (again with that third person) help you find ways to keep... it (whatever 'it" for you may be)... going. Hope you enjoy the episde and thank you for listening to Coach Bennett's Podcast.

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

PS: There's a Two Coach Bennett's merch store now. I know. Flipping awesome.

item.description:
Episode 78. Running Grateful

Thank you. Seems like the best way to start this episode. Because without all of you... this ain't happening. So, thank you. And this entire episode is about giving thanks. Because gratitude, thankfulness... they're superpowers. Superpowers for all athletes but especially runners. That's why this episode is a conversation between you and me. We're going to call attention to some of those people, things and experiences that we are thankful for.

Thank YOU for listening to the podcast. Take care of yourself. Take care of each other. And I'll meet you on that next starting line.

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.itunes:title: Struggle
item.title: Struggle
item.itunes:summary: Episode 77. Struggle. We struggle. We all struggle. We struggle with our running. We struggle with our recovery. We struggle with failure. We struggle with success. We struggle with our responsibilities and obligations. We struggle with our relationships with each other. We struggle with our relationships with ourselves. We struggle with struggling. But do we really understand what it means to struggle. Well, that's the goal of this episode. My hope is that by the end of the show you have...
item.description:
Episode 77. Struggle.

We struggle. We all struggle. We struggle with our running. We struggle with our recovery. We struggle with failure. We struggle with success. We struggle with our responsibilities and obligations. We struggle with our relationships with each other. We struggle with our relationships with ourselves. We struggle with struggling.

But do we really understand what it means to struggle. Well, that's the goal of this episode. My hope is that by the end of the show you have a new point of view of what it means to struggle and a more profound respect for yourself when you do in fact... struggle.

Thank you to everyone that has reached out over the last few weeks and told me that you needed this episode. That's exactly what Coach Bennett's Podcast is for. This podcast is for you.

Cheers,
Coach Bennett
