item.itunes:title: Starting Over
item.title: Starting Over
item.itunes:summary: Episode 76. Starting Over. Starting over. Starting over after an injury or sickness. Starting over after a loss. Starting over after a letdown. Starting over after a win. Starting over after a vacation. Starting off after a season. Starting over after the last run. Starting over after yesterday. Starting over today. Starting over again. This episode is inspired by all of you reaching out telling me you are struggling with starting over. Well, that's what this episode is all about. Sta...
item.description:
Episode 76.  Starting Over.

Starting over. Starting over after an injury or sickness. Starting over after a loss. Starting over after a letdown. Starting over after a win. Starting over after a vacation. Starting off after a season. Starting over after the last run. Starting over after yesterday. Starting over today. Starting over again. This episode is inspired by all of you reaching out telling me you are struggling with starting over. Well, that's what this episode is all about. Starting over and starting over better. So, enjoy the podcast as we break it all down. And yes, there's even a Mindset Minute with coach bennett the greater, tammie bennett of the show up society. Thank you for listening.

Meet you on the starting line,
Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 74. Do You Need To Do More?

It's a question that inevitably gets asked. Do I need to do more? I'm ready for it. Are you ready for the answer(s)? I hope so because the answer(s) are coming your way on this episode. I just want you to know that the answer is yes. But... but... ... but... even though the answer may be yes... it may not be more of what you think. And yes, this is about running and this is not about running. So, settle in and get ready for a great episode. And I'll have you know that there is also a Mindset Minute with the always awesome coach tammie bennett, mindset performance coach extraordinaire.

Cheers and thank you for listening to the show!

Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 73. How To Get Faster.

Oh baby! Fast is fun. I guess that means that faster is funner! So let's talk about how to get to that grammatically problematic place. This episode could have been 4 hours long and still not long enough. Why? Because there is so much that goes into getting faster. We'll discuss all the most essential ingredients to getting faster and we'll set ourselves up to run faster and learn even more about the sport and the training and ourselves. It's a pretty great episode. And we even have a Mindset Minute with mindset performance coach extraordinaire tammie bennett of the show up society. Told fast is fun and faster is funner.

Enjoy the episode and thank you for listening!
Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.title: Why We Need To Recover
item.itunes:summary: Episode 72. Why We Need To Recover. You'd think that convincing people to take the time and initiative to do something like recover would be easy. Well, you'd be wrong and you'd be especially wrong if you thought that convincing runners to recover and recover enough would be easy. So, that's why we're doing this episode. I've talked about how to recover before. Now we're going to talk about why we recover. Strike that. Now we're going to talk about why we NEED to recover. And you better beli...
item.description:
Episode 72. Why We Need To Recover.

You'd think that convincing people to take the time and initiative to do something like recover would be easy. Well, you'd be wrong and you'd be especially wrong if you thought that convincing runners to recover and recover enough would be easy. So, that's why we're doing this episode. I've talked about how to recover before. Now we're going to talk about why we recover. Strike that. Now we're going to talk about why we NEED to recover. And you better believe it when I tell you that this is about running. And this is not about running.   

Cheers and thank you for listening!

Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 71. How To Race Your Best.

Yes, this is about running and this is not about running. But this is definitely about running. And it's also definitely not about running, Doesn't matter if it's a marathon or 5K cross country. Doesn't matter if it's a 10K or a big presentation or an interview or an exam. You can always give your best AND set yourself up to give your best. That's what this episode is all about, It's about YOU. And what YOU can do when you are most wholly you. That's just a really fancy way of me saying when you are at your best you are who you are meant to be. 

Here are some awesome links I promised.

Read any or all of the great writings of Hunter Thompson

Check out Little Heaven by Nick Cutter

Find out how to work with the total rock star coach tammie bennett

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 70. It's OK To Be Nervous.

I hope you know that it's OK to be nervous. Because it is. And that's what this episode is about. Well, that and so much more. It's racing season... and exam season... and big day season... and hard conversation season. What does that mean? It means we always have something to be nervous about if we are living a life fully lived. This episode is all about making sure you know know why you may feel nervous... what being nervous really means... and how confidence and courage play a part in all of this.

Oh, there's more! We have a Mindset Minute with the always extraordinary tammie bennett! Yes, the one and only mindset performance coach extraordinaire of the show up society. So, settle in and enjoy this most wonderful 70th episode of Coach Bennett's Podcast.

item.itunes:title: How To Slow Down Without Slowing Down
item.title: How To Slow Down Without Slowing Down
item.itunes:summary: Episode 69. How To Slow Down Without Slowing Down Sometimes it can feel like we've lost control of a run or our running or our day. Suddenly everything seems to be moving fast... too fast. It's chaotic. It can feel like it's all on fast forward. This episode is how to take back control... release fast forward... and slow down...without slowing down. If you are a coach, an athlete, a teammate, friend, family or supporter then this episode should help when you or someone you care about feels l...
item.description:
Episode 69. How To Slow Down Without Slowing Down

Sometimes it can feel like we've lost control of a run or our running or our day. Suddenly everything seems to be moving fast... too fast. It's chaotic. It can feel like it's all on fast forward. This episode is how to take back control... release fast forward... and slow down...without slowing down. If you are a coach, an athlete, a teammate, friend, family or supporter then this episode should help when you or someone you care about feels like this crazy running thing is all moving too fast. Yes, this is about running. And this is not about running.

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

Here's that link I promised you...

Listen to the always awesome album Reckless by Bryan Adams. It's 40 years old and so much more than Summer of '69.

item.itunes:title: Survive and Advance
item.title: Survive and Advance
item.itunes:summary: Episode 68. Survive and Advance. September. For me... it was my danger zone. When I coached high school I looked at the month of September with a mixture of excitement and anxiety. Mostly anxiety. It was great to get the team back together in school but I also knew that coming back to school meant so much more stress for the athletes. The schedules they had all Summer were about to get completely thrown out. Class. Homework. Sickness. Races. Teachers. Classmates. Sickness. Late lunches. E...
item.description:
Episode 68. Survive and Advance.

September. For me... it was my danger zone. When I coached high school I looked at the month of September with a mixture of excitement and anxiety. Mostly anxiety. It was great to get the team back together in school but I also knew that coming back to school meant so much more stress for the athletes.

The schedules they had all Summer were about to get completely thrown out. Class. Homework. Sickness. Races. Teachers. Classmates. Sickness. Late lunches. Early lunches. Missed breakfasts. Poor sleep. September was hard. And we were supposed to get better while we dealt with all of this?

Everyone has their own September. Could be another month of the year like December or January or June. Could be a danger zone that lasts a week or a day. Sometimes it can feel like all you can do is survive and advance.  So, if you can learn how to handle and deal with and get through your own "September" a better way then you will not just survive and advance you will thrive and move forward. That's what I did with my Septembers. 

Hope you enjoy this episode and thank you to mindset performance coach extraordinaire tammie bennett of the show up society for another great MIndset Minute.

Cheers and thank you for listening!
Coach Bennett

item.content:encoded:
Episode 68. Survive and Advance.

September. For me... it was my danger zone. When I coached high school I looked at the month of September with a mixture of excitement and anxiety. Mostly anxiety. It was great to get the team back together in school but I also knew that coming back to school meant so much more stress for the athletes.

The schedules they had all Summer were about to get completely thrown out. Class. Homework. Sickness. Races. Teachers. Classmates. Sickness. Late lunches. Early lunches. Missed breakfasts. Poor sleep. September was hard. And we were supposed to get better while we dealt with all of this?

Everyone has their own September. Could be another month of the year like December or January or June. Could be a danger zone that lasts a week or a day. Sometimes it can feel like all you can do is survive and advance.  So, if you can learn how to handle and deal with and get through your own "September" a better way then you will not just survive and advance you will thrive and move forward. That's what I did with my Septembers. 

Hope you enjoy this episode and thank you to mindset performance coach extraordinaire tammie bennett of the show up society for another great MIndset Minute.

Cheers and thank you for listening!
Coach Bennett

That's why I brought the best Coach Bennett to the show! That's right! Coach tammie bennett is back! item.description:
Episode 67.  How To Make Running Fun (Again).

You do know that running is supposed to be fun... right? This is a big and important episode. That's why I brought the best Coach Bennett to the show! That's right! Coach tammie bennett is back! She's the mindset performance coach that we all know and love and she's back for this most excellent episode. Running is supposed to be fun! Doesn't mean it's always going to be easy. Doesn't mean it's not supposed to be hard and challenging. It just means that running is supposed to be fun. And maybe those hardest and most challenging parts are supposed to be the most fun parts of it all!

When running is fun it means your ending runs wanting to run again and not dreading the next run. It means that you are being a better teammate to yourself. It means you have a better chance at getting better and having fun doing it. So let’s make sure that running is fun (again). 

I hope you have fun listening to this episode.

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.itunes:title: Suckcess: How To Deal With A Bad Run
item.title: Suckcess: How To Deal With A Bad Run
item.itunes:summary: Episode 66. Suckcess: How To Deal With A Bad Run Those crap runs. They're going to happen now and again. No way around it. Sometimes you're just going to feel sluggish, out of shape, slow. Sometimes you're going to be bored the entire run. Unmotivated. Uninspired. You name it. Sometimes you're going to know why the run was terrible and other times you're going to be lost when it comes to an explanation for the crappy run you just ran. So, what to do? Well, that's what this episode is all...
item.description:
Episode 66. Suckcess: How To Deal With A Bad Run

Those crap runs. They're going to happen now and again. No way around it. Sometimes you're just going to feel sluggish, out of shape, slow. Sometimes you're going to be bored the entire run. Unmotivated. Uninspired. You name it. Sometimes you're going to know why the run was terrible and other times you're going to be lost when it comes to an explanation for the crappy run you just ran.

So, what to do?
Well, that's what this episode is all about!

By the end of this show you'll know just how you can look for and find the suckcess in a run that sucks. Believe me, it's there. And sometimes that suckcess can be the most satisfying, substantial, impactful and insightful kind of success. 

I hope you love the the episode. 
Cheers and thank you for listening!

Coach Bennett

Now, this isn't an episode of me breaking down certain events or races (but we can do that in a future episode if you want!). I give you my biggest takeaways from the games as a whole. And I watched the summer games through a few different lenses. I watched as a fan. I watched as an athlete. I watched as a coach. And I watched as a simple human being. And each of this ...
item.description:
Episode 65. Lessons From The Paris Olympics 

Okay, let's talk about Paris 2024! Because there is so much to talk about. Now, this isn't an episode of me breaking down certain events or races (but we can do that in a future episode if you want!). I give you my biggest takeaways from the games as a whole. And I watched the summer games through a few different lenses. I watched as a fan. I watched as an athlete. I watched as a coach. And I watched as a simple human being. And each of this different viewpoints strengthened the other viewpoints. I hope you enjoy this episode as I roll through some of the great lessons from the Olympic games. And make sure you watch the Paralympics that start August 28th and go through September 8th!

Get all the details for the 2024 Summer Paralympic Games here!

Thank you for listening to the show!

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.title: Hope
item.itunes:summary: Episode 64. Hope. If you run then you're a hopeful person. If you are on a run... you have hope that the run will help you get better in some way. And if you have ever been around a starting line then you have been near one of the most hopeful places on this space rock. And that's a good thing because if you want to run as best as you can then you not only need to have hope you need to know you got it. This is an episode (following episode 62 Confidence and episode 63 Patience) about anot...
item.description:
Episode 64. Hope.

If you run then you're a hopeful person. If you are on a run... you have hope that the run will help you get better in some way. And if you have ever been around a starting line then you have been near one of the most hopeful places on this space rock. And that's a good thing because if you want to run as best as you can then you not only need to have hope you need to know you got it.

This is an episode (following episode 62 Confidence and episode 63 Patience) about another essential ingredient for running success. Whether it's the hope one has before a run or the hope one has when they write a training plan or the hope one has when they start a race it's obvious that hope is always there for the most successful runners and coaches.

I hope you love the episode. See what I did there. I hope so.

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

Patience demands perseverance. And it d...
item.description:
Episode 63. Patience.

All we need is just a little patience. That's what Guns N' Roses said at least. And they were close. We need more than patience. But we do definitely need some patience. This episode is all about one of the essential ingredients to success in running. And it's an important episode because so many runners misconstrue what patience really means. It's not inaction. It's not wasting time. It's not being detached or complacent wither. Patience demands perseverance. And it demands courage and hope. And all of those things help you become a better runner... and a better person too. So, enjoy the episode and please be patient with some of my rambling. You'll be better for it. And thank you to coach tammie of the Show Up Society for another great Mindset Minute. 

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

A few links I think you may enjoy:

I can't refer to Guns N' Roses and Patience and not share the song can I? The answer is no, no I cannot.

Episode 63. Patience.

All we need is just a little patience. That's what Guns N' Roses said at least. And they were close. We need more than patience. But we do definitely need some patience. This episode is all about one of the essential ingredients to success in running. And it's an important episode because so many runners misconstrue what patience really means. It's not inaction. It's not wasting time. It's not being detached or complacent wither. Patience demands perseverance. And it demands courage and hope. And all of those things help you become a better runner... and a better person too. So, enjoy the episode and please be patient with some of my rambling. You'll be better for it. And thank you to coach tammie of the Show Up Society for another great Mindset Minute. 

Cheers,

This one is an important one because success in this most excellent sport of ours is so dependent on your mental and mindful state. And whether or not you are confident plays such a big part on both your mental and mindful state. The problem is that so many athletes and coaches (and parents and teammates) don't understand what confidence really is (and isn't) or how to work on it. Make sure you give this episode a listen and share it with athletes, coaches and parents because we are

item.itunes:title: Mix It Up!
item.title: Mix It Up!
item.itunes:summary: Episode 61: Mix It Up! That's right. Diversity wins. That simple sentence is one of my foundational truths as a coach. And I'm not just talking about making sure that you're running recovery runs and long runs and fartlek runs and tempo runs and intervals and hill workouts. I do mean all that too. But I'm also talking about diversifying your purposes and your goals. And I'm talking about bringing diversity into your mindset and how you measure the success of your running. I'm talking a...
item.description:
Episode 61:  Mix It Up!

That's right. Diversity wins. That simple sentence is one of my foundational truths as a coach. And I'm not just talking about making sure that you're running recovery runs and long runs and fartlek runs and tempo runs and intervals and hill workouts. I do mean all that too. But I'm also talking about diversifying your purposes and your goals. And I'm talking about bringing diversity into your mindset and how you measure the success of your running. I'm talking about making sure that consistency doesn't become constantly. And I'm talking about diversity should lead to flexibility and should strengthen your strengths and strengthen your weaknesses. I talk about so much more but you are just going to need to listen to the episode to find out what else all that is.
There's also a Mindset Minute with the always great coach tammie of the show up society. It's an all around great episode. And yes, this is about running and this is not about running.

Cheers and thank you for listening!
Coach Bennett

item.content:encoded:
Episode 61:  Mix It Up!

That's right. Diversity wins. That simple sentence is one of my foundational truths as a coach. And I'm not just talking about making sure that you're running recovery runs and long runs and fartlek runs and tempo runs and intervals and hill workouts. I do mean all that too. But I'm also talking about diversifying your purposes and your goals. And I'm talking about bringing diversity into your mindset and how you measure the success of your running. I'm talking about making sure that consistency doesn't become constantly. And I'm talking about diversity should lead to flexibility and should strengthen your strengths and strengthen your weaknesses. I talk about so much more but you are just going to need to listen to the episode to find out what else all that is.
There's also a Mindset Minute with the always great coach tammie of the show up society. It's an all around great episode. And yes, this is about running and this is not about running.

Cheers and thank you for listening!
Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 60:  How To Train For Nothing At All.

It gets asked all the time. What should I do? I'm just coming back. I just started. I just finished my big race. I don't want to race. What... should... I... do... because I'm not training for anything? Don't worry! I got you covered! Episode 60 is all about answering one of the most asked questions that I get. We'll talk about the questions you need to ask yourself and the freedom you need to give yourself and the respect you need to give yourself when you are running and training for nothing at all. And we got a Mindset Minute with coach tammie bennett of the show up society. You think I'm going to miss out on having a Mindset Minute when I have access to the mindset coach extraordinaire? No way! So, enjoy the show and please share it with anyone and everyone that needs it.

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.title: Running Isn't Boring (But Some Runners Are)
item.itunes:summary: Episode 59: Running Isn't Boring (But Some Runners Are). I hear it. I hear it all the time. Running is boring. No. No, it's not. Running the same run every day will get boring though. Running the same pace every day will probably get boring. Running with no purpose, running that's always too hard, running that's always too easy... yes, all of those things done consistently can lead to one thinking that running is boring. But, let me tell you this... it ain't the running that's boring. It's t...
item.description:
Episode 59: Running Isn't Boring (But Some Runners Are).

I hear it. I hear it all the time. Running is boring. No. No, it's not. Running the same run every day will get boring though. Running the same pace every day will probably get boring. Running with no purpose, running that's always too hard, running that's always too easy... yes, all of those things done consistently can lead to one thinking that running is boring. But, let me tell you this... it ain't the running that's boring. It's the runner. It's not running that's deciding to do those things! It's the runner!

This episode breaks down how we can become boring runners and more importably how to make sure you don't become a boring runner and how to keep your running fresh. Sounds like a great episode, right? Well, it's even better than what you're thinking! Because there is a Mindset Minute with coach tammie bennett of the Show Up Society!

Enjoy the episode!

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.title: Rest Days
item.itunes:summary: Episode 59. Rest Days Rest Days. Their essential to every athlete. In fact, great running is dependent on great recovery. So, why do we have such a hard time taking the rest we need? Why are we so bad at recovery? Maybe it's because we're looking at rest and recovery the wrong ways. Maybe we're being told the wrong things about work and workloads. Maybe we're not being told the right things. Well, my hope is that this episode helps a little bit. Because we have a rest and recovery problem. ...
item.description:
Episode 59. Rest Days

Rest Days. Their essential to every athlete. In fact, great running is dependent on great recovery. So, why do we have such a hard time taking the rest we need? Why are we so bad at recovery? Maybe it's because we're looking at rest and recovery the wrong ways. Maybe we're being told the wrong things about work and workloads. Maybe we're not being told the right things. Well, my hope is that this episode helps a little bit. Because we have a rest and recovery problem.

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.content:encoded:
Episode 59. Rest Days

Rest Days. Their essential to every athlete. In fact, great running is dependent on great recovery. So, why do we have such a hard time taking the rest we need? Why are we so bad at recovery? Maybe it's because we're looking at rest and recovery the wrong ways. Maybe we're being told the wrong things about work and workloads. Maybe we're not being told the right things. Well, my hope is that this episode helps a little bit. Because we have a rest and recovery problem.

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.itunes:title: Slow Down
item.title: Slow Down
item.itunes:summary: Episode 57: Slow Down. Sometimes you need to slow down to do it right, gain control, maintain form, be present, be your best. And sometimes you need to slow down to speed up, go faster, go further, do more. This is about running, for sure. Easy runs and long runs and speed runs. But this is not about running too. Sometimes you need to slow down to be in the moment, the conversation, the life you are living. This one was inspired by the Olympic Trials but also inspired by the trials w...
item.description:
Episode 57:  Slow Down.

Sometimes you need to slow down to do it right, gain control, maintain form, be present, be your best. And sometimes you need to slow down to speed up, go faster, go further, do more. This is about running, for sure. Easy runs and long runs and speed runs. But this is not about running too. Sometimes you need to slow down to be in the moment, the conversation, the life you are living.

This one was inspired by the Olympic Trials but also inspired by the trials we get through each and every day. Hope you enjoy the episode. 

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.itunes:title: Failing Not Failure
item.title: Failing Not Failure
item.itunes:summary: Episode 56. Failing Not Failure This episode is straight up inspired by the Olympic Trials. There is far more "failing" going on than "succeeding" at the Olympic Trials if we are narrowly defining failing. Well, this episode is about blowing up narrow definitions. Because this is what you need to know... if you are setting big goals, taking on great challenges, chasing after extraordinary achievements.... you are going to come up short from time to time... you are going to stumble... you are ...
item.description:
Episode 56. Failing Not Failure
This episode is straight up inspired by the Olympic Trials. There is far more "failing" going on than "succeeding" at the Olympic Trials if we are narrowly defining failing. Well, this episode is about blowing up narrow definitions. Because this is what you need to know... if you are setting big goals, taking on great challenges, chasing after extraordinary achievements.... you are going to come up short from time to time... you are going to stumble... you are going to fail. That does not make you are a failure. It makes you a badass.

Are you fired up? I'm fired up! Because this is a great episode and we even get a Mindset Minute with the always extraordinary coach tammie bennett of the Show Up Society.

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.itunes:title: Finding Your Pace
item.title: Finding Your Pace
item.itunes:summary: Episode 55. Finding Your Pace It's one of those things that so many runners are looking for... hunting for... searching for. Their PACE! If only there was a map. Well, this episode tries to help you find that elusive pace or should I say elusive paces? Because you have more than one pace. That's because you have more than one effort you run with. And you have more than one purpose to run for. There are Easy Runs & Tempo Runs & Fartlek Runs & Intervals & Long Runs....
item.description:
Episode 55.  Finding Your Pace

It's one of those things that so many runners are looking for... hunting for... searching for. Their PACE! If only there was a map. Well, this episode tries to help you find that elusive pace or should I say elusive paces? Because you have more than one pace. That's because you have more than one effort you run with. And you have more than one purpose to run for. 

There are Easy Runs & Tempo Runs & Fartlek Runs & Intervals & Long Runs. They all ask for different paces. Terrain and weather and life and fitness demand that you adjust your paces. You've got strengths and weaknesses and they both can have an effect on your paces. And all your paces change because of changes in places and head-spaces and faces and races.

Does it seem like a lot? Don't worry! We cover it all in 46 minutes. And we even have time for a Mindset Minute with coach tammie bennett of the Show Up Society and we also open up the mailbag! Episode #55 is a great one. Thank you for checking it out!

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 54: 
Running Streaks

Let's streak! This episode is all about streaking! Sounds so sassy... but it's really just a nerdy little episode about running streaks. I just hit 6 years in a row running at least 5 minutes a day. But this show isn't about my streak. It's about how best to start and continue or end your own running streaks. I go over the pro's and the cons and the best practices and warning signs that a running streak So press play and enjoy the show and maybe even start a Coach Bennett's Podcast listening streak! Just saying... it's an idea. Oh yeah, one more thing. The Mindset Minute with the always great mindset coach extraordinaire tammie bennett is back!

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

Episode 54: 
Running Streaks

Let's streak! This episode is all about streaking! Sounds so sassy... but it's really just a nerdy little episode about running streaks. I just hit 6 years in a row running at least 5 minutes a day. But this show isn't about my streak. It's about how best to start and continue or end your own running streaks. I go over the pro's and the cons and the best practices and warning signs that a running streak So press play and enjoy the show and maybe even start a Coach Bennett's Podcast listening streak! Just saying... it's an idea. Oh yeah, one more thing. The Mindset Minute with the always great mindset coach extraordinaire tammie bennett is back!

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

Here are a few links I mentioned in the episode:

Check out the great coach tammie bennett

item.itunes:summary: Episode 53: How To Run Better In Hot Weather Temperatures are rising... pretty much everywhere... and pretty much at all times of the year. So this topic is an important one. item.description:
Episode 53:   
How To Run Better In Hot Weather 

Temperatures are rising... pretty much everywhere... and pretty much at all times of the year. So this topic is an important one. Because the reality is that we are all going to be dealing with more extreme temperature days. And therefore knowing how best to run (or not run) in those conditions is knowledge that everyone can benefit from. So settle into your chair or get comfortable sitting in traffic or get started on your hike or walk and press play. You'll learn about the importance of the when, where and why of your runs on hot days. You'll also hear about the power of adjustments, the benefits of a great running hat, how sunglasses can keep "Deniro" face away, and why respecting the run on extreme weather days will lead to respecting the runner. There's so much more but I'm tired of typing.

Enjoy the show and thank you for listening!
Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.itunes:summary: Episode 52:: My Coaching Truths Almost ten years ago I was sitting in a Nike conference room in NYC. I was asked about a job. Did I want it? I grabbed a legal pad and wrote down my coaching philosophy. I said if you want me then you need to want this too. item.description:
Episode 52::  My Coaching Truths

Almost ten years ago I was sitting in a Nike conference room in NYC. I was asked about a job. Did I want it? I grabbed a legal pad and wrote down my coaching philosophy. I said if you want me then you need to want this too. A few months later those words I wrote were up on a wall in the old Nike 5th Ave store. They looked good up there. But it’s always been more important for those things to be out there on the roads and trails. On a wall they’re just words. And those truths are meant to be more than read. They are meant to be lived. Ten years down the road… I still got the paper I wrote those words on. And I still believe in what I wrote.

This is episode is all about those truths. I hope you enjoy the show and I hope you listen while you are out there… on the roads… on the trails… on the tracks and treadmills… on all those starting lines… so those truths can be experienced off the wall.

Cheers,
Coach Bennett


Check out the Instagram post about the Truths by tapping here

item.content:encoded:
Episode 52::  My Coaching Truths

Almost ten years ago I was sitting in a Nike conference room in NYC. I was asked about a job. Did I want it? I grabbed a legal pad and wrote down my coaching philosophy. I said if you want me then you need to want this too. A few months later those words I wrote were up on a wall in the old Nike 5th Ave store. They looked good up there. But it’s always been more important for those things to be out there on the roads and trails. On a wall they’re just words. And those truths are meant to be more than read. They are meant to be lived. Ten years down the road… I still got the paper I wrote those words on. And I still believe in what I wrote.

This is episode is all about those truths. I hope you enjoy the show and I hope you listen while you are out there… on the roads… on the trails… on the tracks and treadmills… on all those starting lines… so those truths can be experienced off the wall.

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.itunes:summary: 50 Excuses To Run: Excuses #'s 26-50 We are constantly inundated with excuses to NOT run. So, let's inundate ourselves with excuses TO RUN! item.description:
50 Excuses To Run: Excuses #'s 26-50

We are constantly inundated with excuses to NOT run. So, let's inundate ourselves with excuses TO RUN! In honor of 50 episodes I'm dropping a whole bunch of excuses to run. And by a whole bunch I mean 50 excuses! That's so many excuses that I needed to break this into two different episodes. And I tried my best to be efficient. 50 excuses is just too many excuses for one episode. That's why I needed to break these 50 excuses up into two different episodes. So, sit back and maybe even write a few of these down or write them all down. Believe me... if you run enough you're going to need access to some of these to help get you over the starting line.

Cheers and thank you for listening!

Coach Bennett

PS: Make sure you also listen to  Episode 50: 50 Excuses To Run:  Excuse #'s 1-25

item.itunes:summary: Episode 49. How To Run Consistently Consistency is key. Maybe the biggest key. What does it unlock? Well, just about everything. That's why we're talking about it! If you can run consistently then you can run more and if you can run more better than you can have more better runs and if you can have more better runs then you've got a really great chance at becoming a better runner! item.description:
Episode 49. How To Run Consistently

Consistency is key. Maybe the biggest key. What does it unlock? Well, just about everything. That's why we're talking about it! If you can run consistently then you can run more and if you can run more better than you can have more better runs and if you can have more better runs then you've got a really great chance at becoming a better runner! It makes sense. Believe me.

Cheers and thank you for listening!
Coach Bennett

The Show
(0:00) Welcome and intro
(8:17) Consistently doesn't mean constantly
(13:30) Stop being a jerk
(17:16) Mix things up
(21:40) Stay healthy
(27:37) Be stronger than the run you're doing
(31:37) Do the run you can do...
(33:50) Sometimes the best run is no run
(36:20) Play running. Keep it fun.
(39:23) Let your running be about more than running
(42:53) Ask yourself this question

item.itunes:summary: Episode 48: PB’s - I'm on Team PB's... you may be Team PR's. That's fine. In no way will that diminish your extreme enjoyment of this episode. In fact, I bet you consider this one of the all time best Coach Bennett's Podcast shows. Why? Well, because this topic is awesome! That's why! Personal bests are all about people at their best. Sure, that may mean fastest. That may mean farthest. That may mean highest. But it also means so much more. This episode starts with a discussion of the...
item.description:
Episode 48:   PB’s

- I'm on Team PB's... you may be Team PR's. That's fine. In no way will that diminish your extreme enjoyment of this episode. In fact, I bet you consider this one of the all time best Coach Bennett's Podcast shows. Why? Well, because this topic is awesome! That's why! Personal bests are all about people at their best. Sure, that may mean fastest. That may mean farthest. That may mean highest. But it also means so much more. This episode starts with a discussion of the #PBX concept at the recent Twilight Relays here in Portland, Oregon and the extraordinary  mindset shift that that T&F meet has introduced to the sport. Then the episode talks about how you can expand the definition of PB's so every day offers you the opportunity to be your best. 

Thank you for listening and enjoy the show!
Cheers,
Coach Bennett

Here's the link to the Coach Bennett's Newsletter entitled "Welcome Back to PBX" that I spoke about in episode 48.

item.itunes:summary: Episode 47: Easy Runs - Why do I say what I say to you on an easy run? Why do I tell you to start easy? Why do I tell you to blame me for running slow? Why do I bring up effort before pace? What do I think about pace on an easy run? Why do I make you do system checks for your form and your breathing and your mindset? How do I coach the middle of an easy run? What about the end? Well, these are all great questions! How about I answer them and a few more? Let's do it! Go ahead and...
item.description:
Episode 47:  Easy Runs

- Why do I say what I say to you on an easy run? Why do I tell you to start easy? Why do I tell you to blame me for running slow? Why do I bring up effort before pace? What do I think about pace on an easy run? Why do I make you do system checks for your form and your breathing and your mindset? How do I coach the middle of an easy run? What about the end? Well, these are all great questions! How about I answer them and a few more? Let's do it!

Go ahead and head out for an easy run or go for a hike or walk or just listen to this in traffic or on the train. Because this episode will benefit your easy runs whether you are on one of them or just getting ready for one in your future.

Cheers and thank you for listening!
Coach Bennett

Episode 46:   Stop That! Start This.

- We do so many things that slow us down or set us back. And we don't do so many things that would set us up to move ahead. So, let's stop that all that nonsense. And let's start this a better way. We'll go over golden oldies like sleep and hydration. We'll get (not so) new age with mindset and better risk taking. We'll hit a classic like idiotic training and so much more. Hope you enjoy... no... more than that... I hope you love this episode.

Cheers and thank you for listening!
Coach Bennett

item.itunes:title: Questions For YOU To Answer
item.title: Questions For YOU To Answer
item.itunes:summary: Episode 45: Questions For YOU To Answer That's right! We're flipping the script. I'm asking the questions and you are answering them. So, get ready to use that big brain of yours and ponder some sticklers and stumpers. This is a really fun episode. At least I think it is. And it's also some great training for your runs. A run should be a great conversation between your mind and body after all. And great conversations are often made up of questions and stories. This run will demand both...
item.description:
Episode 45:  Questions For YOU To Answer

That's right! We're flipping the script. I'm asking the questions and you are answering them. So, get ready to use that big brain of yours and ponder some sticklers and stumpers. This is a really fun episode. At least I think it is. And it's also some great training for your runs. A run should be a great conversation between your mind and body after all. And great conversations are often made up of questions and stories. This run will demand both... from you!

I also think that you'll need (and want) to spend some time after the episode pondering and processing the questions I give you and the answers you give back. Especially since I ask you a bunch of followup questions to every main question I ask. Anyway, that's why I'm going to list out the main questions from the episode. 

So, here they are:

Why do you want to run?
Where is your favorite place to run?
What time of year is your favorite time of year to run?
Track or trails?
What's the worst life advice you've ever been given?
Who was/is the best teammate you've ever had?
What's the hardest thing you've ever done?
Do you dance differently when you are alone?
If you were tasked with making one meal for people you cared about what would it be?
If you could set one big goal for your running right now what would it be?
Who in your life do you most believe needs an invitation to a starting line?

Cheers and thank you for listening!

Coach Bennett



Send us a message

Be sure to send any and all questions and comments to the mailbag:

item.itunes:title: Running Isn't Complicated
item.title: Running Isn't Complicated
item.itunes:summary: Episode 44: Running Isn't Complicated The episode is really called Running Isn't Complicated... Runners Are. But I figured brevity would be the best course to take here. The goal of episode 44 is pretty simple... some would say... wait for it... uncomplicated. So much of running is not complicated. It's a simple activity. But runners are indeed complicated. And if there is one thing that complicated people do really well is overcomplicate the things around them. That's okay! Be...
item.description:
Episode 44:   Running Isn't Complicated

The episode is really called Running Isn't Complicated... Runners Are. But I figured brevity would be the best course to take here. The goal of episode 44 is pretty simple... some would say... wait for it... uncomplicated.

So much of running is not complicated. It's a simple activity. But runners are indeed complicated. And if there is one thing that complicated people do really well is overcomplicate the things around them. That's okay! Because we can simplify. We can learn to uncomplicate.  We can figure things out.

(And yes, I know uncomplicate is not a real word.)

In this episode some of the uncomplicated parts of running that get complicated by us humans we'll talk about and uncomplicate. Now that's a complicated sentence!

So enjoy the podcast as such uncomplicated topics as getting started, how to ramp up training, motivation. running form... they all get discussed.

Cheers and thank you for listening!
Coach Bennett

Send us a message

item.description:
Episode 43 -  Mindset Matters: Goals

You wanted more Mindset Matters! Ask and ye shall receive. That's right! The one and only mindset coach extraordinaire tammie bennett (lower case letters per her demand/request) is back for another episode of Mindset Matters. This time we talk GOALS. Big goals. Small goals. Short term goals. Long term goals. New goals. Old goals. We talk about all of them. And believe me when I tell you that there is no one better to talk GOALS with than coach tammie of the show up society. So go lace up those trainers or set up the cushions an pillows on your couch or settle in to that traffic laden commute because this is a fantastic episode. And it's sure to set you up to start, adjust, reach and celebrate all those goals you've got in your future. 

You can learn more about coach tammie and follow and reach out to her here:

Check out the show up society here.

Follow coach tammie on instagram here.

Cheers and thank you for listening!

Coach Bennett

This is episode takes you behind the line - If You Can't Do The Run You Want To Do... Do The Run You Can Do - and gives you the background to one of Coach Bennett's most popular phrases. Behind The Line will be a reoccurring episode so be sure to reach out and ask for your favorite line to be covered in a future show.

item.description:
Episode 41:   Being Hurt

This episode is inspired by so many of you asking me what has inspired the way I talk about and handle injuries.  Well, we're going to need to run down one of my memory lanes. This episode begins with a story about my junior year in high school and my first serious injury that just happened to happen when I was first getting serious about running.

The lessons from those 6 months when I was a (very cute... dare I say, adorable) sixteen year old are still being taught to me today. I talk about the need to work with experts, be an active participant in your healing, stay involved with the sport and your teammates, continue to cross starting lines, do those things while you're recovering that you couldn't do when you were running, and talk and write about and share your hurt.

I hope thing episode about me being hurt and what I learned from being hurt can help you... whether you are hurt or not.

Cheers and thank you for listening.

Coach Bennett
*********************

Something I think you'll enjoy reading:

Check out this story I wrote about that first run back after the injury I got at 16.

item.description:
Episode 40:   Answering Your (Mostly About) Running Questions

Well, I put together 14... maybe it's 15... could actually be 13 of the most popular questions that get sent my way and I answer them (as best as I can) on this episode.  Questions like do I need to run a marathon, how many days should I run, how do I start running, and why do you have that mustache?
You know... the questions I get asked the most.
So, sit back or start your run or get walking because this is a great episode. And thank you for sending in so many questions! Keep them coming!

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.itunes:title: How To Make Adjustments So You Run Better
item.title: How To Make Adjustments So You Run Better
item.itunes:summary: Episode 39: How To Make Adjustments So You Run Better Coaching isn't about writing a training plan. It's about making adjustments to the training plan. And this episode is all about those adjustments that sometimes need to be made. This is an important episode because so many athletes believe making adjustments to a run, training plan, or workout schedule is a sign of weakness or lack of commitment. That's just silly balderdash! So listen up because this episode is going to give you...
item.description:
Episode 39:   How To Make Adjustments So You Run Better

Coaching isn't about writing a training plan. It's about making adjustments to the training plan. And this episode is all about those adjustments that sometimes need to be made. This is an important episode because so many athletes believe making adjustments to a run, training plan, or workout schedule is a sign of weakness or lack of commitment. That's just silly balderdash!

So listen up because this episode is going to give you the knowledge and the confidence to make adjustments when and if needed. And you'll realize that what makes a training plan or run great are the proper adjustments to that training plan or run.

Enjoy the episode and thank you for listening!

Cheers,
Coach Bennett


Send us a message

Be sure to send any and all questions and comments to the mailbag:

item.content:encoded:
Episode 39:   How To Make Adjustments So You Run Better

Coaching isn't about writing a training plan. It's about making adjustments to the training plan. And this episode is all about those adjustments that sometimes need to be made. This is an important episode because so many athletes believe making adjustments to a run, training plan, or workout schedule is a sign of weakness or lack of commitment. That's just silly balderdash!

So listen up because this episode is going to give you the knowledge and the confidence to make adjustments when and if needed. And you'll realize that what makes a training plan or run great are the proper adjustments to that training plan or run.

Enjoy the episode and thank you for listening!

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.itunes:summary: Episode 38: Tips To Make Your Running Suck Sometimes its just easier to tell someone what not to do than it is to tell them what to do. And for this episode that's what we're doing. Well, unless you want your running to suck. Then we are actually telling you what to do. (0:00) Intro (4:00) U2 concert in Las...
item.description:
Episode 38: Tips To Make Your Running Suck

Sometimes its just easier to tell someone what not to do than it is to tell them what to do. And for this episode that's what we're doing. Well, unless you want your running to suck. Then we are actually telling you what to do. This was a fun one to make and covers a lot of different aspects of running. So, settle in and get ready to make your running better by not doing things that will make your running suck!

(0:00) Intro
(4:00) U2 concert in Las Vegas
(9:32) Why make this episode?
(11:40) Start your runs too fast if you want your runs to suck
(15:26) Never run fast if you want your running to suck
(20:03) Make running boring to make running suck
(24:00) Don't realize that running is a sport and you'll make running suck
(28:27) Make running complicated and you'll make running suck
(31:30) Make your running only about the numbers and you'll make running suck
(37:15) Don't change as a runner, athlete, coach or person and running will suck
(40:34) Make running a punishment if you want your running to suck
(43:44) Belittle yourself and/or others and your running will suck
(52:54) END

Cheers and thank you for listening and sharing!
Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 37: Recover Like A Badass

Great running is dependent on great recovery so it makes sense to talk about how you can recover like a badass. And if you can recover like a badass you will be increasing the possibility of running like one! So, listen up! Because it's time to learn how to recover better... and therefore run better. By the way, recovering like a badass is about more than recovering from running. But you knew that already, right? 

(0:00) Introduction
(2:30) Start with what you've already started
(3:45) Sleep Better
(5:55) Less Booze = Better Snooze
(8:40) Chill Out
(11:20) Let Darkness Reign
(13:05) Develop the Habit of Getting Sleepy
(20:42) Hydration Motivation
(26:13) Fueling: It's Not What You Keep Out That Matters... It's What You Put In
(30:45) Laugh Your Way To Better Recovery
(32:20) Stress. Deal With It

Thank you for listening to the show!

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

Read Recover Like A. Badass from Coach Bennett's Newsletter and make sure you subscribe to the newsletter!

item.itunes:summary: Episode 36: Getting Comfortable Being Comfortable Yes, you read it correctly. Sure, you probably hear about how important it is to learn how to get comfortable being uncomfortable. But you know what's even more important? Getting comfortable being comfortable. It's not only more important it's also harder! You read that correctly too! It's easier to get people to run too hard than easy enough. It's harder to get people to run easy! And that's because it's just so hard for so many of ...
item.description:
Episode 36:  Getting Comfortable Being Comfortable

Yes, you read it correctly. Sure, you probably hear about how important it is to learn how to get comfortable being uncomfortable. But you know what's even more important? Getting comfortable being comfortable. It's not only more important it's also harder! You read that correctly too!

It's easier to get people to run too hard than easy enough. It's harder to get people to run easy! And that's because it's just so hard for so many of us to be comfortable simply being comfortable. We think we are not running hard enough. We believe we are not trying hard enough. We doubt that what we are doing is enough if we're comfortable doing it. I made this episode because I want to end that nonsense. So, settle in and enjoy the episode.

And special thanks to the great mindset coach tammie bennett of the show up society for gracing us with another mindset minute to close out the episode.

You can check out coach tammie and the show up society here. 

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.itunes:title: Mistakes I've Made So You Don't Have To
item.title: Mistakes I've Made So You Don't Have To
item.itunes:summary: EP 35: Mistakes I've Made So You Don't Have To I don't believe that you need to fall down to know that it hurts. I don't believe that you need to make a mistake to learn from one. I believe that we can learn from each others mistakes and stumbles and falls. That's why I'm sharing a whole bunch of my mistakes in the hope that doing so will keep you from making the same gaffes. Some of these mistakes are seemingly obvious ones. Well, the obvious mistakes are obvious because they happe...
item.description:
EP 35:  Mistakes I've Made So You Don't Have To

I don't believe that you need to fall down to know that it hurts. I don't believe that you need to make a mistake to learn from one. I believe that we can learn from each others mistakes and stumbles and falls. That's why I'm sharing a whole bunch of my mistakes in the hope that doing so will keep you from making the same gaffes.

Some of these mistakes are seemingly obvious ones. Well, the obvious mistakes are obvious because they happen the most. The vast majority of mess ups that I share are directly related to running . Some are tangentially related. And there's even one that's just a grooming mistake. But there is no need for any of you to make that mistake either.

I hope you enjoy the episode and have some fun at my expense. And if this episode helps you have better runs or keeps a goatee off your face... then I've done my job. 

Cheers,
Coach Bennett


(0:00) Intro
(2:34) You don't need to run on ice. I've done it.
(6:50) You don't need to do a beer mile or chocolate milk mile. I did both.
(10:30) I've run with a phone in my hand... you shouldn't do that
(13:40) Don't run in shoes that don't fit. I've done it.
(17:13) You don't need to not have toilet paper when you need it.
(19:40) You don't need to run a race you shouldn't run. I did.
(23:35) Don't tell yourself you can't race because you're not in great shape. I did that.
(25:52) Don't run in a thunderstorm. Idiots do that. I did that. I was an idiot.
(27:39) You don't need to hang out in the sun unprotected for hours. I did it. It sucks.
(32:21) I've run when I was sick. I've run when I was hurt. You don't need to do that.
(34:47) You don't need to have a goatee. Ever. I had one once. It was a disaster.
(36:32) Don't eat a pepperoni stromboli the night before an early morning race.
(38:45) Thought I could get hydrated during a run. I couldn't. You probably can't either.
(40:14) I used to say I hated running. It wasn't true and it hurt my running. Don't do that.

Send us a message

Be sure to send any and all questions and comments to the mailbag:

Coach Bennett’s Podcast

item.itunes:title: How To Become Your Own Best Coach
item.title: How To Become Your Own Best Coach
item.itunes:summary: Episode 34: How To Become Your Own Best Coach You really want to become a better runner? Work on becoming a better coach! Become your own best coach. Do that and you'll run better, smarter, happier and with more joy. You'll also give yourself the best chance to run faster and further. In this episode I'll tell just how you can start to work on becoming a better coach to and for yourself. And if I do my job really well this all ends with you eventually demoting me and promoting you. That's th...
item.description:
Episode 34: How To Become Your Own Best Coach

You really want to become a better runner? Work on becoming a better coach! Become your own best coach. Do that and you'll run better, smarter, happier and with more joy. You'll also give yourself the best chance to run faster and further. In this episode I'll tell just how you can start to work on becoming a better coach to and for yourself. And if I do my job really well this all ends with you eventually demoting me and promoting you. That's the goal every coach should have by the way. Their athletes should need them a little less every time they meet. Because every athlete should also be a coach.
And every coach should be a teacher and student!

Enjoy episode 34.
 
(00:00) Introduction
(3:50) The week that was and is
(11:00) Why I made this episode
(15:45) Get to know the athlete (that means YOU!)
(20:35) Understand what it means to coach
(28:16) Believe the athlete today can become the athlete you see tomorrow
(31:10) Flexibilty and consistency not constantly and insistency
(35:15) Don't be a jerk
(38:40) Now I know you. Now I get to know you more.
(41:16) You got to be honest for this to truly work.
(43:10) Celebrate. And celebrate some more.
(45:08) Tell the athlete (you) thank you. 
(46:52) Best Runner Ever invitation

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

Links I mentioned in the episode or just some links I think you'll like:

All the details and information and stuff you need to learn about and then register for the group coaching experience known as Best Run Ever: Winter Edition

item.title: How To Run With Low Motivation
item.itunes:summary: Episode 33: How To Run With Low Motivation. It's that time of year. Motivation can wane. Inspiration can dim. How do I know it's that time of year? Because those things happen during any and every time of year. That's why we're talking low motivation! And there is a lot to talk about. So, settle in and get cozy because this is a topic that everyone can benefit from. The Rundown of the Episode (0:00) Introduction (1:44) Snow Storm Memories (6:25) Why This Topic? (9:35) First, talk ...
item.description:
Episode 33:  How To Run With Low Motivation.

It's that time of year. Motivation can wane. Inspiration can dim. How do I know it's that time of year? Because those things happen during any and every time of year. That's why we're talking low motivation! And there is a lot to talk about. So, settle in and get cozy because this is a topic that everyone can benefit from.


The Rundown of the Episode
(0:00) Introduction
(1:44) Snow Storm Memories
(6:25) Why This Topic?
(9:35) First, talk to yourself
(11:20) Look at your training
(12:40) Lack of diversity kills motivation
(18:15) Working Too Hard Doesn't Work
(20:35) Weather Plays A Role
(23:50) When you're not running...
(26:20) Where to look for motivation when it's missing
(30:28) A few simple ways to mix/motivate things up
(33:20) A little challenge for you
(34:55) Let the run be about more than running
(37:33) Pencils out. Why do you run?
(39:10) Sometimes the best run is no run
(40:25) Best Runner Ever

item.content:encoded:
Episode 33:  How To Run With Low Motivation.

It's that time of year. Motivation can wane. Inspiration can dim. How do I know it's that time of year? Because those things happen during any and every time of year. That's why we're talking low motivation! And there is a lot to talk about. So, settle in and get cozy because this is a topic that everyone can benefit from.


The Rundown of the Episode
(0:00) Introduction
(1:44) Snow Storm Memories
(6:25) Why This Topic?
(9:35) First, talk to yourself
(11:20) Look at your training

(18:15) Working Too Hard Doesn't Work
(20:35) Weather Plays A Role
(23:50) When you're not running...

item.description:
Episode 32:  How To Get Over Bad Runs And Races

Bad runs. They happen. Bad races. They happen too. It's just part of the deal. If you cross enough starting lines you're going to have a few crap runs and races. But even though everyone has a clunker of a run or a dumpster fire of a race we still struggle to deal with them and move on from them.

Well, this episode is all about how to deal with and handle and get over and move on and learn from and take advantage bad runs and bad races.

That's not all! Coach Bennett somehow works Miami Vice and Jai Alai into the episode. And there's a Mindset Minute with coach tammie of the show up society! Hold on! There's more! The mailbag gets opened up too! Yes, this is an extraordinary episode.

(0:00) Start
(0:45) Intro to the episode
(1:35) Welcome
(8:40) Be Honest
(11:30) Sometimes (sh)it just happens
(15:16) It's okay to not be okay
(21:45) The two questions to ask
(27:14) Learn from it
(34:12) No regrets is stupid
(39:25) The better you get... the harder it gets to get better
(41:15) Embrace your weaknesses
(44:46) Measure success as many ways as you can
(46:22) Mindset Minute with coach tammie of the show up society
(47:55) Best Runner Ever: Winter Edition program
(48:40) Mailbag

Some links for you to check out and enjoy:

Learn all about BEST RUNNER EVER: WINTER EDITION

Jai Ala from the opening credits of Miami Vice

Check out coach tammie and the show up society

Read the latest Coach Bennett's Newsletter (and please subscribe)

Check out the latest Instagram reel from Coach Bennett

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 31:  Why You Should Play Running

We play basketball. We play football. We play tennis and play field hockey. We play cricket and we play volleyball. Why don't we play running? We used to. When we were kids we did. What happened? Well, a lot of things happened. Can we go back to playing running? Yes! Will playing running help us run, run more and run better? Yes and yes and yes! How? You got to listen to this episode of Coach Bennett's Podcast to find out.

(0:00) Welcome & Introduction to Episode
(4:45) Definition of Sport
(8:36) We Played Running as Kids
(15:08) Some Coaching Truths About Playing Running
(22:49) How To Keep Running Play
(33:08) Some Ways To Play Running
(40:45) Training Should Be Written in Pencil 

Here are a few links for you to check out! 

Read  Coach Bennett Bennett's Newsletter entitled "Wanna Play Some Running?"

Read Coach Bennett's Instagram Post from 2021 about Play Running

Cheers and thank you for listening!
Coach Bennett

item.content:encoded:
Episode 31:  Why You Should Play Running

We play basketball. We play football. We play tennis and play field hockey. We play cricket and we play volleyball. Why don't we play running? We used to. When we were kids we did. What happened? Well, a lot of things happened. Can we go back to playing running? Yes! Will playing running help us run, run more and run better? Yes and yes and yes! How? You got to listen to this episode of Coach Bennett's Podcast to find out.

(0:00) Welcome & Introduction to Episode
(4:45) Definition of Sport
(8:36) We Played Running as Kids
(15:08) Some Coaching Truths About Playing Running

Episode 30.    
Start Your New Year Running!

This is an episode for anyone and everyone. Coach Bennett goes over the most essential aspects of starting the New Year (or any other time of year) running and running better.

Introduction (0:00)
Running Is Supposed to Be Fun  (5:28)
Be Patient (11:22)

Flexibility Leads To Consistency (19:58)

Celebrate More (46:00)

Cheers and thank you for listening!

item.description:
Episode 29: My Big 2023 Year In Review.

It's a great time of year to look back at the year that was. So, I'm looking back at my 2023 and that's inspiring me to look ahead to 2024. I talk about what I did that I'm proud of as well as what I should have and could have done better in 2023 and hopefully will do better in 2024. I tell you what the best books I read were and the best movies I watched. I ramble and I roll. Through it all I hope that you have fun listening to the review and more importantly are inspired to do your own 2023 review.

Check out these links

Read Wool by Hugh Howey
Read The Return by Hisham Matar

Read the books of Lyndsay Faye
Read Robert E. Lee and Me by Ty Seidule

Read Coach Bennett's Newsletter (and subscribe too)

Check out Best Run Ever: Winter Edition

Cheers and thank you for listening to the podcast!

item.itunes:title: Five Workouts That Are Really FUN (And Really HARD)
item.title: Five Workouts That Are Really FUN (And Really HARD)
item.itunes:summary: Episode 28. Five Workouts That Are Really FUN (And Really HARD) NERDS! Get excited because this one is a doozy! And by doozy I mean that this episode is straight fun. I'm going to tell you about 5 workouts that are super fun and really hard... or is it really fun and super hard? Maybe it's both. Whatever. Doesn't matter. What matters is that this episode will give you 5 workouts that you can use to mix up your training, challenge yourself, have a good time, and brag to your running b...
item.description:
Episode 28.   Five Workouts That Are Really FUN (And Really HARD)

NERDS! Get excited because this one is a doozy! And by doozy I mean that this episode is straight fun. I'm going to tell you about 5 workouts that are super fun and really hard... or is it really fun and super hard? Maybe it's both. Whatever. Doesn't matter. What matters is that this episode will give you 5 workouts that you can use to mix up your training, challenge yourself, have a good time, and brag to your running buddies about after you do them.

I talk about the Michigan, Endless Fartlek, 40/30, Quarters Up & Down, Split Intervals, 

The Michigan: 2K Tempo, 1600m at ~10K Pace, 2K Tempo, 1200m at ~5K Pace, 2K Tempo, 800 @ ~3K Pace, 2K Tempo, 400M at 1500m/Mile Pace or faster. (2K Tempo can be shorter. As an example I've had athletes do 1K of Tempo instead of 2K.)

Endless Fartlek: Fartlek for unknown amount of time with unknown durations of hard and easy. You just don't know how long you got to go hard or can go easy.  (Try the Funky Fartlek in the Nike Run Club app.)

40/30: Continuous run (ideally on track) with pace changing every 200 meters. The workout is called 40/30 because the athletes at the University of Oregon would run a 40 second 200m and then go straight into a 30 second 200m. The workout continued until you can no longer successfully run 40/30. You can personalize this by making the 200m times based on YOUR Tempo Run pace and YOUR Mile/1500m Pace.

Quarters Down & Up: You can play with this workout in your own way. My example... Start with a 400 at your Tempo Run pace. Recover for same duration as that 400. Next interval is a 400m ONE SECOND FASTER. Recovery is same duration as that 400. Next interval is a 400m ONE SECOND FASTER. Recovery is same duration as that 400. You keep getting one second faster until you can't. Then you start running 400's ONE SECOND SLOWER. Recovery is same duration as that 400.  You keep slowing down by ONLY ONE SECOND until you can't and you slow down more than one second. That ends the workout.

Split Intervals: You simply split up an interval into different paces. For example... you do a 1000m interval and an 800m interval and a 400 Interval. The 1K could be split up as a 200m at 10K pace into 400m at 5K pa

item.itunes:title: Simple Strength Work That Works For Runners
item.title: Simple Strength Work That Works For Runners
item.itunes:summary: Episode 27 - Simple Strength Work For Runners That Works You asked. I'm delivering. You wanted to hear about some basic strength training that you can do to help you run better... help you become a better runner... help you be a stronger athlete while you run. We talk planks, we talk flamingos and pretzel rods and bicycles and fire hydrants and a bunch more simple exercises you can do to help you achieve what you want to achieve. item.description:
Episode 27 - Simple Strength Work For Runners That Works

You asked. I'm delivering. You wanted to hear about some basic strength training that you can do to help you run better... help you become a better runner... help you be a stronger athlete while you run. 

We talk planks, we talk flamingos and pretzel rods and bicycles and fire hydrants and a bunch more simple exercises you can do to help you achieve what you want to achieve.

Yes, those weird names are just nicknames I use for the different exercises. I'm sure there are far more technical names for these same exercises. But they're boring names and besides I don't know them or knew them and forgot them.

And don't worry... there is an offer from me to do some videos that can accompany this episode if enough of you ask for them. 

Here are some sweet links for you to investigate:

Check out all the awesome details for Best Runner Ever: Winter Edition. This 4 week program starts in February 2024 and is going to be fantastic. We hope you can be a part of Best Runner Ever!

Have you subscribed to Coach Bennett's Newsletter? I hope so! Because a new one is dropping in just a few days! Sign up so you can get the new newsletter delivered straight to you inbox.

Listen to the latest (and maybe greatest but definitely loudest) Coach Bennett's Mixtape. You wanna rock? I got you covered!

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

Send us a message

Be sure to send any and all questions and comments to the mailbag:

item.title: What I Learned From Running 2000 Days In A Row
item.itunes:summary: Episode 26: What I Learned From Running 2000 Days In A Row This one was... well, 2000 days in the making. What started as an attempt to get more consistent has become something I'm really proud of and at the same time something that I'm okay with ending... if it ever becomes just a streak. This episode is all about what I've learned over the last 5 1/2 years running every... single.. day. Hope you enjoy the episode and maybe even get inspired to go for a run yourself. item.description:
Episode 26:  What I Learned From Running 2000 Days In A Row

This one was... well, 2000 days in the making. What started as an attempt to get more consistent has become something I'm really proud of and at the same time something that I'm okay with ending... if it ever becomes just a streak.

This episode is all about what I've learned over the last 5 1/2 years running every... single.. day. Hope you enjoy the episode and maybe even get inspired to go for a run yourself. Cheers and thank you for listening.

Here are a few links for you to go nuts on:

item.title: Super Special Bonus Episode: NXN

The 22 best girls teams and 50 best individuals meet in Portland, Oregon to all take the same starting line and race for the glory. The 22 best boys teams and 50 best individuals meet in Portland, Oregon to all take the same starting line and race for the glory. Two starting lines. Two races. But so much more than ...
item.description:
Bonus Episode:  Super Special NXN Episode!

NXN (NIKE Cross Nationals) is my favorite race. Period. It's my favorite.

NXN is the US high school XC nationals championships. The 22 best girls teams and 50 best individuals meet in Portland, Oregon to all take the same starting line and race for the glory. The 22 best boys teams and 50 best individuals meet in Portland, Oregon to all take the same starting line and race for the glory.

Two starting lines. Two races. But so much more than that is going on. And this episode my attempt to share a little history about the meet but more importantly what is going on during the race so YOU can watch the race and fully enjoy the pure epicness (is that a real word?) that is NXN.

If you run... then you need to listen to this episode. Because you should be a fan of this race and these young athletes. They will inspire you and motivate you. And NXN is a badass celebration of our sport.

Here are some GREAT links for you to further enjoy NXN:

Latest US Girls Teams Rankings according to Dyestat
Latest US Boys Teams Rankings according to Dyestat
Latest US Girls Individuals Rankings according to Dyestat
Latest US Boys Individuals Rankings according to Dyestat
 
Watch the LIVE STREAM here on Runnerspace 12/2/23 (Show starts at 9 AM PST)

The Stride Report Boys Team National Rankings
The Stride Report Girls Team National Rankings

Send us a message

Be sure to send any and all questions and comments to the mailbag:

item.description:
Episode 25. Grateful  Running.

Thank you for listening to this episode. Feels good to say thank you. Feels good to hear it too. And this episode is all about thankfulness and gratitude. Thankfulness and gratitude for running. Runners. Runs. The community around the sport. The culture. The lessons and opportunities. Grateful and thankful for so much. And I think being grateful and thankful is a super for a runner. So, listen to this episode and get in on the action. By the end of the episode you'll hear a whole bunch of what I'nm grateful for and I'll ask you to come up with a whole bunch of things that you're grateful for.

There's also a BIG announcement about the next edition of BEST RUNNER EVER.

Yup, a new Best Runner Ever is on the way. This February the Best Runner Ever: Winter Edition will go live. 4 weeks. 2 zoom calls each week. A special t-shirt that I'm going to design and I'm entirely too excited about that. Anyway, check out the details starting Thursday on either of the coach bennetts social media.

And the extra cool thing here is that from Thursday November 23 through November 27 there is an opportunity to get a special discount and Links below.

Cheers... and THANK YOU for listening!

Coach Bennett

A few great links for you to check out...

Follow Coach Bennett on Threads. Definitely starting to post there more and more.

Check out coach tammie and the show up society... because you are going to find all the details about BEST RUNNER EVER.

Read the latest Coach Bennett's Newsletter here.

item.description:
Ep. 24  - Fantastic Tips For Better Winter Running

Not one. Not two. Nay! Five fantastic tips are heading your way! And these five fantastic tips are all about better Winter running. So join Coach Bennett as he shares a few ways you can help make this a Winter you run through and not just get through. This terrific episode (I'm biased but it's true) also has a Mindset Minute with coach tammie of the show up society.

Some truly stellar links for you to enjoy:

Listen to the song with the longest title in the Pearl Jam catalogue

Read Citius Magazine for some truly great coverage of the NCAA XC Championships

Check out the great coach tammie and the show up society

Watch this little video of a gorgeous snowy run I went on last year... and notice the hats, gloves, and jacket.

Read (and subscribe) to Coach Bennett's Newsletter

Check me out on cameo for any pep talks, birthday wishes, pre-race messages, post race congratulations

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 23. Mindset Matters: All About Racing
 
Do we have a special episode for you? Oh baby yes! We've got Mindset Coach Extraordinaire tammie bennett (she loves lower case letters) of The Show Up Society. We asked for questions last week and you responded with so many questions we literally had to delay this episode because it took too long to record and edit and upload.  That's my way of blaming you for this dropping episode late and also celebrating you for making this episode a reality. So, sit back... or run with with this... or walk with it.... or sit in traffic with this fantastic episode. And thank you for listening!

Here are some links for you to freak out about... in a good way...

Check out coach tammie and her one on one coaching opportunities

Listen to the Race Day episode of Coach Bennett's Podcast

Get some information on the show up society community

Read Coach Bennett's Newsletter

Read one of the all time great "letters" to parents about their kids running

Cheers and thank you for listening!
Coach Bennett

item.description:
Episode 22.  Could You Be A Runnerd?

This episode was inspired by a Coach Bennett's Newsletter from September 2023 that was inspired by an Instagram post I wrote in 2015.  And after feedback from the readers of the newsletter asking for more information about runnerdism I finally caved and... well... here we are.

This episode will not only define what a Runnerd  actually is but will also celebrate what it means to be a runnerd and even give some background on Coach Bennett's journey from an adorable and wildly charismatic kid to a handsome and wildly charismatic runnerd.  So, calling all runnerds! Get ready to listen to an episode that is all about you and your passion and your love. It's about you. Because you nerd out about running and that is glorious because that makes you a runnerd!

Hope you love the episode and thank you for listening!

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

Here are the links I promised you in the episode:

Read the September 2023 Coach Bennett's Newsletter It's Great To Be A Runnerd

Here is my 2015 Instagram post about being a Runnerd

Welcome To Wrexham series on Hulu

Listen to the new Album History Books by Gaslight Anthem on Spotify

Listen to the new Album History Books by Gaslight Anthem on Apple Music

Check out the coach tammie bennett and her Show Up Society

Check out all the cool stuff that Nike NYC is putting on this big race weekend

Be sure to order your special cameo message from Coach Bennett for your favorite teammate, friend, family member or even yourself. Pre-race messages, post race congratulations, big event celebratory messages, birthday wish

Send us a message

Be sure to send any and all questions and comments to the mailbag:

item.content:encoded:
Episode 22.  Could You Be A Runnerd?

This episode was inspired by a Coach Bennett's Newsletter from September 2023 that was inspired by an Instagram post I wrote in 2015.  And after feedback from the readers of the newsletter asking for more information about runnerdism I finally caved and... well... here we are.

This episode will not only define what a Runnerd  actually is but will also celebrate what it means to be a runnerd and even give some background on Coach Bennett's journey from an adorable and wildly charismatic kid to a handsome and wildly charismatic runnerd.  So, calling all runnerds! Get ready to listen to an episode that is all about you and your passion and your love. It's about you. Because you nerd out about running and that is glorious because that makes you a runnerd!

Hope you love the episode and thank you for listening!

Cheers,
Coach Bennett

Here are the links I promised you in the episode:

Read the September 2023 Coach Bennett's Newsletter It's Great To Be A Runnerd

Here is my 2015 Instagram post about being a Runnerd

Welcome To Wrexham series on Hulu

Listen to the new Album History Books by Gaslight Anthem on Spotify

Listen to the new Album History Books by Gaslight Anthem on Apple Music

Check out the coach tammie bennett and her Show Up Society

Check out all the cool stuff that Nike NYC is putting on this big race weekend

Be sure to order your special cameo message from Coach Bennett for your favorite teammate, friend, family member or even yourself. Pre-race messages, post race congratulations, big event celebratory messages, birthday wish
